summary: Comprehensive audit of the MAXAM Tires rebuild: docs vs reality, every subsystem, salvageability

result.summary: None of the five documents accurately describes the system that exists on disk today (2026-06-10). CLAUDE.md is a March-era snapshot in which essentially every "current state" and "what's next" claim is now false — it actively misdirects anyone (human or agent) who reads it as a contract. IMPLEMENTATION-PLAN.md and DESIGN-SYSTEM.md are superseded historical artifacts; wp-page-parity.md is the only doc close to current reality, but it is written entirely in the Rubber*/rubbermorphism vocabulary the user has since banned, and it names four components that have never existed. The actual architecture — Notion sync pipeline writing JSON snapshots, offline build, central CSS system — is documented nowhere except code comments. Verdict: rewrite CLAUDE.md and README from scratch, archive IMPLEMENTATION-PLAN.md and DESIGN-SYSTEM.md, salvage wp-page-parity.md as the living Phase-4 spec after a naming/correction pass, and write a new architecture doc.
result.stats: 5 docs audited, ~60 factual claims checked. CLAUDE.md: 17 of ~22 current-state/next-step claims false or stale. DESIGN-SYSTEM.md: 14+ inventory claims superseded in 3 days. wp-page-parity.md: 4 phantom components, 1 false done-claim, otherwise ~90% verified accurate. 1 referenced doc (docs/PROJECT-PLAN.md) missing. 3 conflicting MySQL ports (10017/10018/10023). Reality on disk: 39 .astro components (16 documented), 9 page routes, 1050 built HTML pages (19 documented), Notion snapshot synced 2026-06-10 with 351 products/972 articles (docs say "create Notion DBs next").

findings.title: CLAUDE.md 'Current State' section is entirely fictional — every operational claim is March-era stale
findings.evidence: CLAUDE.md:9 "Current State (as of 2026-03-23)". CLAUDE.md:18 "Mock data: src/data/mock-tires.ts (4 placeholder tires)" — file exists but contains 39 tires (regenerated by scripts/generate-mock-data.ts) and is dead code: no page imports it (only src/data/mock-articles.ts:7 imports it, itself unimported). CLAUDE.md:19 "Pages: [locale]/tires/index ... [locale]/tires/[industry]/[slug]" — no tires/ routes exist; actual routes are src/pages/[locale]/products/index.astro, products/[industry]/[slug].astro, plus resources/, contact/, design/ (9 page files total). CLAUDE.md:20 "Build passes: 19 static pages generated" — dist/ contains 1050 HTML files. CLAUDE.md:31-36 "What's next: 1. Create Notion databases ... 3. Wire up src/lib/notion.ts to replace mock data layer" — all done months ago, differently: src/lib/notion.ts never created; instead src/lib/notion/{client,fetchers,query,unwrap}.ts + scripts/sync-from-notion.ts; src/data/notion-content/_manifest.json shows "syncedAt": "2026-06-10T15:56:08.524Z" with 351 products, 972 articles, 390 documents. CLAUDE.md:27 "all 124 unique English tires" — scripts/output/tires-en.json has 117 entries today. CLAUDE.md:29 "1,028 TablePress spec tables" — tablepress-tables.json has 1063. CLAUDE.md:42 "scripts/db.ts — connection helper (root:root@localhost:10017)" — scripts/db.ts:6 reads "port: 10023". Still-true claims: i18n exports (src/lib/i18n.ts:14,19,40 export t/getDir/localePath), translations files, src/types/index.ts types, BaseLayout, components/global files, slice-*.json extracts.
findings.planRef: CLAUDE.md is itself the contract doc; violates its own purpose of describing current state
findings.recommendation: Rewrite CLAUDE.md from scratch. It is the file agents read first; today it points them at routes, data layers, and next-steps that have not existed for ~2.5 months. New version should state: snapshot-based Notion pipeline (npm run sync), products/ routes, the no-Tailwind and no-Rubber decisions, the central CSS architecture, and the product-specs open loop.
findings.verifierNote: Every citation re-checked and exact. CLAUDE.md:9 reads 'Current State (as of 2026-03-23)'; :18 claims '4 placeholder tires' while src/data/mock-tires.ts actually contains 39 tires (39 slug entries in the tires array starting line 53; regenerated by scripts/generate-mock-data.ts:236) and is dead code — the only importer is src/data/mock-articles.ts:7 ('import { industries } from ./mock-tires'), which itself is imported nowhere. :19's tires/ routes do not exist; actual src/pages has 9 files (products/, resources/, contact/, design/, index). :20's '19 static pages' vs 1050 HTML files in dist/ (verified by find|wc). :27 '124 unique English tires' vs 117 entries in scripts/output/tires-en.json. :29 '1,028' vs 1063 in tablepress-tables.json. :42 claims port 10017; scripts/db.ts:6 reads 'port: 10023,'. src/lib/notion.ts never created; src/lib/notion/{client,fetchers,query,unwrap}.ts exist. _manifest.json: syncedAt 2026-06-10T15:56:08.524Z, products 351, articles 972, documents 390 — all exact. Still-true claims (i18n exports at i18n.ts:14/19/40, translations, BaseLayout) also verified as stated.

findings.title: All five docs enshrine Tailwind v4 as the stack while user decision #1 bans it — and the code is still mid-migration
findings.evidence: README.md:8 "[Tailwind CSS v4](https://tailwindcss.com/) — Utility-first CSS"; CLAUDE.md:5 "Astro 6 + Notion CMS + Tailwind CSS v4 + Netlify" and CLAUDE.md:12 "Tailwind v4 (via @tailwindcss/vite)"; docs/IMPLEMENTATION-PLAN.md:5,29,38 ("refine Tailwind config"); docs/DESIGN-SYSTEM.md:118-119 "Type scale (Tailwind defaults, used throughout)"; docs/wp-page-parity.md:372 "Bootstrap grid — CSS framework (we use Tailwind v4)". Reality: src/styles/global.css:1 is still `@import 'tailwindcss';`, package.json still has tailwindcss 4.2.1 + @tailwindcss/vite 4.2.1, astro.config.mjs:3,14 still wires `tailwindcss()`, and 19-20 .astro files still carry utility classes, e.g. src/components/global/Footer.astro:16 `class="max-w-[var(--container-max)] mx-auto px-4 py-12"`, src/pages/[locale]/resources/index.astro:37 `class="type-filter-btn px-4 py-1.5 text-sm rounded-full border border-maxam-gray-300 ..."`. Meanwhile 26 semantic stylesheets exist under src/styles/components/ + src/styles/pages/ per the decision.
findings.planRef: User decision #1 (NO Tailwind; semantic classes + central CSS only)
findings.recommendation: No document records the no-Tailwind decision, so every doc contradicts it. Record the decision in the rewritten CLAUDE.md/README, then finish the migration (Footer, Header, MegaNav, LanguageSwitcher, ProductCard, ArticleCard, FeaturedCard, IndustryCard, Hero, SpecTable, ProductsViewer, NotionBlocks, NotionRichText, BaseLayout, resources pages, contact <style> block) and remove the tailwind import/deps. Until then the docs describe the violation as the intended stack.
findings.verifierNote: Doc cites all exact: README.md:8, CLAUDE.md:5 and :12, IMPLEMENTATION-PLAN.md:5/29/38, DESIGN-SYSTEM.md:118-119, wp-page-parity.md:372 ('Bootstrap grid — CSS framework (we use Tailwind v4)'). Code reality verified: src/styles/global.css:1 is @import 'tailwindcss'; package.json has tailwindcss 4.2.1 (line 28) + @tailwindcss/vite 4.2.1 (line 25); astro.config.mjs:3 imports and :14 wires tailwindcss(). Two small corrections: (1) the quoted Footer utility div 'max-w-[var(--container-max)] mx-auto px-4 py-12' is at Footer.astro:15, not :16; (2) '26 semantic stylesheets under components/ + pages/' is actually 29 (26 in src/styles/components/ + 3 in src/styles/pages/) — the finding undercounts, which strengthens rather than weakens it. My own utility-class regex finds 21 .astro files still carrying utilities (finding said 19-20 — same ballpark, list includes Footer, Header, MegaNav, ProductsViewer, BaseLayout, resources pages, contact, etc.). resources/index.astro:37 quote is exact. Not overstated.

findings.title: wp-page-parity.md — the active Phase-4 spec — is written entirely in the banned Rubber*/rubbermorphism vocabulary and directs future work to create more of it
findings.evidence: docs/wp-page-parity.md:5 "rebuilding each WP page in our rubbermorphism system"; :380-391 prescribes new components "RubberHeroSection, RubberLogoGrid, RubberFeatureSlider, RubberTestimonialSlider, RubberFAQ ... RubberGlobalCTA, RubberResourceCenterStrip, RubberContent2Col, RubberContent3ColSpecial, RubberVideoEmbed"; :399 "Page rebuilds in rubbermorphism vocabulary". Reality matches the doc, not the decision: 17 Rubber* component files exist (src/components/ui/RubberButton.astro ... RubberTextarea.astro, src/components/global/RubberHeader.astro), BaseLayout.astro:32 renders `<RubberHeader locale={locale} variant="dark" />`, 18 of 26 stylesheets in src/styles/components/ use rubber- prefixed classes (e.g. Footer.astro:15 `class="rubber-footer mt-auto"`, ProductsViewer.astro:95 `class="rubber-chip"`), and global.css:50 labels the token block "Rubbermorphism: physical layering system".
findings.planRef: User decision #2 (NO Rubber/rubber prefix on components or CSS classes)
findings.recommendation: wp-page-parity.md is the most accurate and most useful doc — salvage it, but it needs a systematic rename pass (Rubber* -> semantic names) before any Phase-4 work proceeds, or it will keep manufacturing decision-#2 violations. Pair it with the actual component rename in code.
findings.verifierNote: Verified exactly: wp-page-parity.md:5 'page in our rubbermorphism system' (sentence spans lines 4-5); :380-391 is the '### New section components (rubbermorphic)' list naming RubberHeroSection, RubberLogoGrid, RubberFeatureSlider, RubberTestimonialSlider, RubberFAQ, RubberStatsBar, RubberGlobalCTA, RubberResourceCenterStrip, RubberContent2Col, RubberContent3ColSpecial, RubberVideoEmbed; :399 '### Page rebuilds in rubbermorphism vocabulary'. Code: exactly 17 Rubber*.astro files (16 in src/components/ui/ + src/components/global/RubberHeader.astro); BaseLayout.astro:32 renders <RubberHeader locale={locale} variant="dark" /> (exact); 18 of 26 src/styles/components/*.css files contain .rubber- selectors (grep -l count); ProductsViewer.astro:95 has class="rubber-chip" data-variant="category" (exact); global.css:50 is the '── Rubbermorphism: physical layering system ─' comment (exact). One off-by-one: 'rubber-footer mt-auto' is Footer.astro:14, not :15.

findings.title: The real architecture is documented nowhere — only code comments describe the Notion sync pipeline, snapshot data layer, and CSS system
findings.evidence: The only accurate architecture description on disk is the comment in src/lib/data.ts:1-9: "Backed by JSON snapshots in src/data/notion-content/*.json that are produced by `npm run sync` (scripts/sync-from-notion.ts). The build is fully offline — Astro never makes Notion API calls." None of the 5 docs mentions: scripts/sync-from-notion.ts, the 14 snapshot files in src/data/notion-content/, the blocks/ sidecar pattern, the ~50 scripts in scripts/ (create-notion-*-db.ts, import-*-to-notion.ts, link-*.ts, incremental-sync.ts...), package.json:11-12 sync/sync:fast commands, or the .env NOTION_TOKEN requirement (.env.example). The known open loop — product-specs built from the WP export, not Notion — is documented only in scripts/build-product-specs.ts:6 "Source: scripts/output/tires-<locale>.json (the WP migration output)". CLAUDE.md:31-36 instead documents a pre-Notion world.
findings.planRef: User decision #3 (Notion snapshot pipeline is the canonical content flow; product-specs is a known open loop)
findings.recommendation: Write a short ARCHITECTURE/PIPELINE doc (or section in rewritten CLAUDE.md): sync command, snapshot layout, data.ts as the only read path, scripts/output/ as one-time migration artifacts, and the product-specs exception with its planned resolution. This is the single highest-leverage doc to write.
findings.verifierNote: Verified the negative claim directly: grep for 'sync-from-notion', 'notion-content', and 'npm run sync' across CLAUDE.md, README.md, and all docs/*.md returns zero matches. src/lib/data.ts:1-9 header comment quote is exact ('Backed by JSON snapshots... The build is fully offline — Astro never makes Notion API calls'). src/data/notion-content/ has exactly 14 .json snapshots plus the blocks/ sidecar dir; scripts/ has exactly 50 .ts files; .env.example documents NOTION_TOKEN; scripts/build-product-specs.ts:6 quote 'Source: scripts/output/tires-<locale>.json (the WP migration output)' is exact, and its header lines 1-4 confirm the product-specs open loop. One correction: the sync/sync:fast npm scripts are at package.json:13-14, not :11-12 — the commands themselves ('tsx scripts/sync-from-notion.ts' and '--without-blocks') are as described.

findings.title: wp-page-parity.md names four homepage components that do not exist on disk
findings.evidence: docs/wp-page-parity.md:26 "**Hero** — `RubberHero`", :31 "**Promo card pair** — `RubberPromoPair`", :50 "**Sustainability strip** — `RubberSustainabilityStrip`", :61 "**Newsletter CTA** — `RubberNewsletterCTA`". Grep of src/ finds zero matches for any of these names — they appear only in the doc. The homepage (src/pages/[locale]/index.astro) builds these sections inline with Section/SectionHeader/RubberChip/RubberInput/RubberButton plus classes in src/styles/pages/home.css (e.g. index.astro:149 `<h2 class="strip-heading">`). Contrast: the doc's other component references (RubberIndustryCard, RubberProductCard, RubberTestimonialCard, RubberArticleCard) do exist and are imported at index.astro:6-12, and the featured slugs at :44 `['agrixtra-85', 'ms453', 'ms600', 'ms709']` match the doc exactly.
findings.planRef: docs/wp-page-parity.md homepage spec (sections 1, 2a, 5, 7)
findings.recommendation: During the wp-page-parity salvage pass, replace the four phantom names with what actually renders (Section variants + page CSS), or extract those sections into real components if that was the intent — right now the doc claims component boundaries the code never drew.
findings.verifierNote: All four doc citations land exactly: wp-page-parity.md:26 'RubberHero', :31 'RubberPromoPair', :50 'RubberSustainabilityStrip', :61 'RubberNewsletterCTA' (confirmed by grep -n). Grep of src/ for all four names (word-bounded, so RubberHero does not false-match RubberHeader) returns zero matches. Homepage builds these sections inline: index.astro imports Section/SectionHeader/Grid/Rubber{IndustryCard,ProductCard,ArticleCard,TestimonialCard,Button,Chip,Input} at lines 2-12, and index.astro:149 is <h2 class="strip-heading"> (exact). One off-by-two: the featured slugs array ['agrixtra-85', 'ms453', 'ms600', 'ms709'] is at index.astro:42, not :44; it does match the doc's slug list at wp-page-parity.md:46 exactly.

findings.title: wp-page-parity.md falsely marks the resources filter bar as migrated ("uses RubberChips now")
findings.evidence: docs/wp-page-parity.md:174 "**Our equivalent:** ✓ exists; filter bar uses RubberChips now". Reality: src/pages/[locale]/resources/index.astro imports only RubberArticleCard (line 3) — no RubberChip — and renders the filter pills as raw Tailwind buttons: line 37 `class="type-filter-btn px-4 py-1.5 text-sm rounded-full border border-maxam-gray-300 hover:border-maxam-blue hover:text-maxam-blue transition-colors"`, line 48 `class="industry-filter-btn px-3 py-1 text-xs rounded-full ..."`. The products page did get chip integration (ProductsViewer.astro:95 `class="rubber-chip" data-variant="category"`), so the doc likely conflated the two pages.
findings.planRef: docs/wp-page-parity.md section 5; user decisions #1 and #5 (one chip atom)
findings.recommendation: Correct the doc and add the resources filter bar to the migration backlog — it is the last filter UI still on the legacy inline-utility pattern, and the doc currently hides that work.
findings.verifierNote: Exact at every cite. wp-page-parity.md:174 reads '**Our equivalent:** ✓ exists; filter bar uses RubberChips now; featured-resource card pattern is **missing**.' Reality: src/pages/[locale]/resources/index.astro:3 imports only RubberArticleCard (no RubberChip anywhere in its imports, lines 1-7); line 37 is the raw-Tailwind type-filter button class and line 48 the industry-filter button class, both quoted verbatim in the finding. The contrast claim also holds: ProductsViewer.astro:95 uses class="rubber-chip" data-variant="category" data-size="md", so the products page did get the chip atom while resources did not. The doc statement is demonstrably false today.

findings.title: DESIGN-SYSTEM.md claims to be the 'source of truth ... updated as the system evolves' but was abandoned after 2 days — its core inventory is now wrong
findings.evidence: docs/DESIGN-SYSTEM.md:3 "Audit captured 2026-06-07. This document is the source of truth for what UI exists ... Updated as the system evolves." Superseded claims: :30 "Button — Missing. 5 inline variants" (src/components/ui/RubberButton.astro + src/styles/components/button.css exist); :32 Input/Select/Textarea "Missing" (RubberInput/RubberSelect/RubberTextarea/RubberField + css exist); :48 "Breadcrumb — Inline" (RubberBreadcrumb.astro exists); :46 "TestimonialCard — Missing ... never rendered" (RubberTestimonialCard.astro exists, rendered at index.astro:119); :47 "EventCard — Missing" (RubberEventCard.astro exists); :44 "OfficeCard — Inline on contact page" (RubberOfficeCard.astro imported at contact/index.astro:3); :54 "ContentGrid | src/components/ui/ContentGrid.astro" (file does not exist; renamed Grid.astro); :7 "~16 components" (39 .astro components exist); :107 industry colors "Stored per-industry in Notion (not in CSS)" (global.css:21-30 now defines --color-industry-*); :118 "No custom typography token layer beyond Tailwind" (global.css:33-41 defines --font-size-*); :127-132 "missing as tokens: radius scale, shadow scale, z-index scale, transition timing" (all defined at global.css:67-116). Its section-7 proposal was built: src/pages/[locale]/design/index.astro:5-6 "Gated by env var: only builds when MAXAM_SHOW_DESIGN_GALLERY=1", getStaticPaths returns [] otherwise (line 47) — at /[locale]/design/, not the proposed /_design. Still-accurate claims: data counts (21 testimonials, 6 events, 390 documents match _manifest.json), and the legacy-component inconsistencies in section 4 still hold for the unmigrated files.
findings.planRef: docs/DESIGN-SYSTEM.md:3 (its own maintenance promise)
findings.recommendation: Archive it with a header pointing to the design gallery page, which the doc itself argued should replace it ("always reflects what's actually shipping"). Do not try to maintain both — the doc lost that race in 48 hours.
findings.verifierNote: All ~14 citations re-checked; every line number lands. findings.title: Two contradictory design-token systems are documented simultaneously — WP palette (CLAUDE.md/IMPLEMENTATION-PLAN) vs what global.css actually defines
findings.evidence: CLAUDE.md:63-66 and docs/IMPLEMENTATION-PLAN.md:85-97 specify "Sky Blue #e5f1f9, Dark Blue #1c2858, Green #009042, Orange #f79425, Red #9e0b0f, Yellow #dcb127 ... Helvetica Neue ... Container: 1170px ... pill buttons (28px radius)" with Tailwind keys (sky-blue, dark-blue, brand-green...). None of these values exists anywhere in src/styles/: global.css:10-18 defines a different palette (--color-maxam-blue #0077c8, --color-maxam-black #1a1a1a, gray scale), global.css:48 `--container-max: 1280px`, global.css:146 `font-family: system-ui, -apple-system, 'Segoe UI', Roboto, sans-serif` (no Helvetica Neue, no public/fonts dir), global.css:105 `--radius-pill: 999px`. Only #0077c8 survives from the WP palette. wp-page-parity.md:416 explicitly disclaims the WP look ("not a 1:1 visual copy"), directly contradicting CLAUDE.md's token section. The font question is also a never-closed loop: IMPLEMENTATION-PLAN.md:38 "[ ] Step 2: Copy fonts ... from WP theme" still unchecked, DESIGN-SYSTEM.md:281 lists "Typography choice" as open.
findings.planRef: CLAUDE.md:63-66, docs/IMPLEMENTATION-PLAN.md:85-97 vs docs/wp-page-parity.md:416 and src/styles/global.css
findings.recommendation: Pick one written answer: the rubbermorphism-era tokens in global.css are the implemented reality. Delete the WP token tables from CLAUDE.md when rewriting, and explicitly close (or schedule) the typography decision — three docs still imply Helvetica Neue is coming.
findings.verifierNote: Exact throughout. CLAUDE.md:63-66 lists the WP palette/Helvetica Neue/1170px/28px-pill; docs/IMPLEMENTATION-PLAN.md:85-97 has the same token table with Tailwind keys (sky-blue, dark-blue, brand-green...) ending at :97 'Typography: Helvetica Neue family. findings.title: Homepage ships links to pages that don't exist — doc TODOs from wp-page-parity confirmed still open in production build
findings.evidence: docs/wp-page-parity.md:43 "View all → `/testimonials` *(broken link — page not built)*" and :53 "CTA → `/sustainability` *(broken link — page not built)*" are accurate today: src/pages/[locale]/index.astro:115 `viewAllHref={localePath(locale, '/testimonials')}` and the sustainability Section (index.astro:143+) link to routes with no corresponding files under src/pages/[locale]/ (only contact/, design/, products/, resources/, index.astro exist), and dist/en/ contains no testimonials/ or sustainability/ directories. These 404s ship on every locale's homepage.
findings.planRef: docs/wp-page-parity.md:43,53,65-69 (homepage outstanding work)
findings.recommendation: Either build the /testimonials and /sustainability pages (parity doc already specs them as Phase-4 priorities) or temporarily drop/repoint the links — the doc flagged this 2 days ago and it remains user-visible breakage.
findings.verifierNote: Confirmed and slightly strengthened. One off-by-one: the testimonials broken-link note is at wp-page-parity.md:42, not :43 (:53 for sustainability is exact). index.astro:115 viewAllHref={localePath(locale, '/testimonials')} is exact; the sustainability CTA is at index.astro:154 within the Section starting at :141 (finding's ':143+' is approximately right). Nuance checked that could have refuted it: the sustainability href is Notion-driven via pageText(homePage, 'sustainability.cta_href', '/sustainability'), so the synced content could in principle repoint it — but src/data/notion-content/pages.json sets cta_href to '/sustainability' anyway, so fallback and CMS value agree. Verified the breakage actually ships in all three locales: dist/en/index.html, dist/ar-ae/index.html, and dist/zh-hant/index.html each contain href="/<locale>/testimonials" and href="/<locale>/sustainability", while no testimonials/ or sustainability/ directory exists in any dist/<locale>/ (and no such source pages exist under src/pages/[locale]/).

result.summary: The routing system is a single, clean [locale]/ tree — suspicion (a) is REFUTED: src/pages/en/, src/pages/ar-ae/, src/pages/zh-hans/ do not exist in the working tree and never existed in any commit (git log --all over those paths returns nothing; no zh-hans/zh-hant page dirs ever existed). However, the tree that does exist has a critical cross-locale routing fracture: industry hub paths are generated from EN slugs for all three locales while every link uses per-locale slugs, producing 404 hubs and orphaned product subtrees in ar-ae and zh-hant — verified in the actual dist/ build. On top of that, the homepage ships two known-broken links (/testimonials, /sustainability) in all locales, the language switcher fabricates URLs that 404 for ~50 EN-only articles, and the page templates are saturated with Tailwind utilities, a scoped style block, and Rubber*-prefixed imports in direct contradiction of recent user decisions. CLAUDE.md describes a route tree (/tires/) that no longer exists.
result.stats: 9 route templates → 1050 built pages (root 1, en 383, ar-ae 333, zh-hant 333; per locale: 123 product pages, 208–258 resource pages, 1 contact). 7 public page types vs 10 WP page types with no route (parity doc list). Broken: 5 nav-linked industry-hub URLs 404 (2 ar-ae, 3 zh-hant), 5 noindex redirect-stub hubs, 2 homepage links ×3 locales to nonexistent routes, ~50 EN-only resource URLs break the language switcher, ~36 product-page breadcrumbs 404. Dead: 2 mock data files, 1 impossible fallback URL shape. Design gallery route correctly env-gated but untracked in git.

findings.title: Industry hub routes generated from EN slugs for all locales while all links use localized slugs — 404 hubs and orphaned product trees in ar-ae/zh-hant
findings.evidence: src/pages/[locale]/products/[industry]/index.astro:18-27: `const industries = getAllIndustries('en'); ... for (const locale of locales) { for (const industry of industries) { paths.push({ params: { locale, industry: industry.slug } }); } }` — but src/data/notion-content/industries.json has per-locale slugs that differ: ar-ae has `rubber-tracks-ar-ae`, `tbr-ar-ae`; zh-hant has `agricultural-tractor-tires`, `rubber-tracks-zh-hant`, `tbr-tires-zh-hant` (vs en `rubber-tracks`, `tbr-tires`, `agricultural-tires`). All navigation links use the localized slug: src/components/global/Header.astro:103 `href={localePath(locale, \`/products/${ind.slug}/\`)}`, MegaNav.astro:46 same, RubberIndustryCard.astro:23 `const href = \`/${locale}/products/${industry.slug}/\`;`. Verified in build: dist/ar-ae/index.html contains `href="/ar-ae/products/tbr-ar-ae/"` and `href="/ar-ae/products/rubber-tracks-ar-ae/"` but dist/ar-ae/products/tbr-ar-ae/ has NO index.html (only 8 orphaned product subdirs); dist/zh-hant/index.html links `agricultural-tractor-tires/` (17 orphaned product subdirs, no hub index). Meanwhile the EN-slug hubs built for those locales are dead noindex stubs because `getIndustryBySlug(locale, enSlug)` fails → Astro.redirect (index.astro:32-34): dist/zh-hant/products/agricultural-tires/index.html = `<meta http-equiv="refresh" content="2;url=/zh-hant/products"><meta name="robots" content="noindex">`. Product detail breadcrumbs also 404: [slug].astro:115 `href: localePath(locale, \`/products/${primaryIndustry.slug}\`)` → /ar-ae/products/tbr-ar-ae on ~8 ar-ae and ~28 zh-hant tire pages. Notion promo content compounds it: page-promos.json:48,84 `"ctaHref": "/products/rubber-tracks"` for ar-ae/zh-hant lands on the redirect stub, not the localized hub.
findings.planRef: User decision 6 (locale layouts identical in structure across en/ar-ae/zh-hant); user decision 3 (sync-from-notion.ts is the content pipeline — it does not normalize industry slugs across locales)
findings.recommendation: Pick one canonical slug set. Either (a) normalize localized industry slugs to the EN slug in scripts/sync-from-notion.ts (URL slugs should not be translated/suffixed per-locale), or (b) generate getStaticPaths per-locale from getAllIndustries(locale). Then add a build-time internal-link checker so a nav link to a non-generated route fails the build.
findings.verifierNote: Re-checked every citation. src/pages/[locale]/products/[industry]/index.astro:18-27 reads exactly as quoted (getStaticPaths uses getAllIndustries('en') for all locales; redirect at :32-34). industries.json slug divergence verified by dumping the file: ar-ae has rubber-tracks-ar-ae/tbr-ar-ae, zh-hant has agricultural-tractor-tires/rubber-tracks-zh-hant/tbr-tires-zh-hant vs en rubber-tracks/tbr-tires/agricultural-tires. Link sites verified: Header.astro:103, MegaNav.astro:46, RubberIndustryCard.astro:23, all fed by getAllIndustries(locale) (Header.astro:16, MegaNav.astro:16). Build evidence verified: dist/ar-ae/index.html links /ar-ae/products/tbr-ar-ae/ and /ar-ae/products/rubber-tracks-ar-ae/; dist/ar-ae/products/tbr-ar-ae/ has exactly 8 product subdirs and NO index.html; dist/zh-hant/products/agricultural-tractor-tires/ has 17 subdirs and no index.html; dist/zh-hant/products/agricultural-tires/index.html is the exact noindex refresh stub quoted; dist/ar-ae/products/rubber-tracks and tbr-tires are the same stubs. Breadcrumb at [slug].astro:115 exact, and products ARE generated under localized industry slugs ([slug].astro:22 uses tire.industries[0].slug per locale), so the orphaned trees are real. Two small corrections: (1) ar-ae broken-breadcrumb pages number 11, not ~8 (8 in tbr-ar-ae + 3 in rubber-tracks-ar-ae); zh-hant's 28 (17+8+3) was exact. (2) page-promos.json line numbers: the /products/rubber-tracks ctaHref is at line 12 (zh-hant) and line 48 (ar-ae); line 84 is the en entry, which resolves fine — the underlying claim (ar-ae and zh-hant promos land on redirect stubs) still holds, and dist/ar-ae/index.html does contain the slash-less href="/ar-ae/products/rubber-tracks" from the promo.

findings.title: LanguageSwitcher fabricates cross-locale URLs by blind segment swap — guaranteed 404s for EN-only articles and mismatched industry slugs; no hreflang alternates
findings.evidence: src/components/global/LanguageSwitcher.astro:13-19: `function switchLocalePath(targetLocale, path) { const segments = path.split('/').filter(Boolean); if (locales.includes(segments[0])) segments.shift(); return \`/${targetLocale}/${segments.join('/')}\`; }` — no check that the target route exists. Build proof of asymmetry: dist/en/resources has 258 pages vs 208 in ar-ae and zh-hant (50 EN-only articles, e.g. /en/resources/45-degree-lug-design exists, /ar-ae/resources/45-degree-lug-design does not). Same failure on industry hubs with locale-suffixed slugs (switching from /en/products/tbr-tires/ to ar-ae lands on a noindex redirect stub). BaseLayout.astro contains no hreflang/alternate links (grep for 'hreflang|alternate' returns nothing), so search engines get no cross-locale mapping either.
findings.planRef: User decision 6 (no English-text-matching logic / identical locale structure) — structurally the switcher assumes URL-identical content across locales, which the data does not guarantee
findings.recommendation: Make the switcher route-aware: fall back to the target locale's section index (e.g. /ar-ae/resources/) when the exact slug has no counterpart, and emit hreflang alternates from the same mapping in BaseLayout.
findings.verifierNote: Code citation exact: src/components/global/LanguageSwitcher.astro:13-19 is the quoted switchLocalePath (split/filter/shift/rejoin, no existence check), used as <option value> with a window.location.href redirect (lines 29-44). Build asymmetry verified exactly: dist/en/resources has 258 index.html pages vs 208 in both ar-ae and zh-hant; dist/en/resources/45-degree-lug-design exists while the ar-ae/zh-hant counterparts do not. dist/ar-ae/products/tbr-tires/index.html confirmed as a noindex meta-refresh stub, so switching locale on /en/products/tbr-tires/ does land on the stub. hreflang claim verified: grep for 'hreflang|alternate' across src/ returns only an unrelated code comment in RubberProductCard.astro:17; BaseLayout.astro has neither. Not overstated.

findings.title: All 8 page templates contradict the no-Tailwind / no-scoped-style / no-Rubber decisions at scale
findings.evidence: Scoped style block: src/pages/[locale]/contact/index.astro:146-182 `<style> .rubber-contact-form { position: relative; ... }</style>`. Utility classes throughout pages: resources/index.astro:37 `class="type-filter-btn px-4 py-1.5 text-sm rounded-full border border-maxam-gray-300 hover:border-maxam-blue ..."` and :223-225 JS toggling utilities `btn.classList.toggle('bg-maxam-blue', isActive)`; resources/[slug].astro:43 `class="max-w-3xl mx-auto px-4 py-10"`; contact/index.astro:65 `class="max-w-[var(--container-max)] mx-auto px-4 py-10"`. Tailwind still wired: astro.config.mjs:3,14 `import tailwindcss from '@tailwindcss/vite'` / `plugins: [tailwindcss()]`; src/styles/global.css:1 `@import 'tailwindcss';`; package.json devDeps `"tailwindcss": "4.2.1"`. Rubber*-prefixed components imported by every page: [locale]/index.astro:6-12 (RubberIndustryCard, RubberProductCard, RubberArticleCard, RubberTestimonialCard, RubberButton, RubberChip, RubberInput), contact/index.astro:3-9, products/[industry]/[slug].astro:7-10, resources/[slug].astro:4-6. Only [locale]/index.astro and products pages are mostly semantic-class; contact and resources pages are predominantly utility-class markup.
findings.planRef: User decisions 1 (semantic classes + central CSS only, no scoped <style>, no utility classes) and 2 (no Rubber prefix) — both violated in the page layer; CLAUDE.md:5,12 still prescribes 'Tailwind CSS v4' (stale doc pulling in the opposite direction)
findings.recommendation: Treat the migration as half-finished: home/products pages show the target pattern (semantic classes + src/styles/pages/*.css). Finish converting contact, resources index, and resource detail; delete the contact page's scoped style into src/styles/components/; rename Rubber* components; then remove tailwindcss from astro.config.mjs/global.css/package.json so regressions are impossible.
findings.verifierNote: Every citation verified verbatim: contact/index.astro:146-182 scoped <style> defining .rubber-contact-form (plus utility classes at :65 and throughout); resources/index.astro:37 utility-pill button classes and :220-232 JS toggling bg-maxam-blue/text-maxam-white/border-maxam-blue (cited as 223-225 — the toggles are at 223-225 and repeated 229-231); resources/[slug].astro:43 'max-w-3xl mx-auto px-4 py-10'; astro.config.mjs:3,14 tailwindcss vite plugin; global.css:1 @import 'tailwindcss'; package.json devDeps tailwindcss 4.2.1 + @tailwindcss/vite 4.2.1. Rubber* import counts per template: home 7, contact 7, design 15, products/[industry]/index 2, products/[industry]/[slug] 4, resources/index 1, resources/[slug] 3. One nuance to the title: the 8th template, products/index.astro, is itself fully compliant (semantic classes only, zero Rubber imports, no style block) — it violates only transitively via ProductsViewer.astro, which imports RubberProductCard (line 25) and uses utility classes (lines 59, 80). So 'all 8' is accurate for what the pages render but slightly overstated for the templates' own markup (7 of 8 directly). Severity and substance stand; the finding's own text already concedes home/products pages are 'mostly semantic-class'.

findings.title: Contact page hardcodes editorial office data with inline per-locale strings — including Simplified Chinese in the zh-hant locale
findings.evidence: src/pages/[locale]/contact/index.astro:21-61: comment 'Office data extracted from WordPress (block-3-column-special)' followed by `const officeRegionNames: Record<string, Record<Locale, string>> = { northAmerica: { en: 'North America', 'ar-ae': 'أمريكا الشمالية', 'zh-hant': '北美' }, eu: { ..., 'zh-hant': 'Maxam Tire（欧盟）' }, amea: { ..., 'zh-hant': 'Maxam Tire（亚洲、中东和非洲）' } }` plus a full `offices` array of addresses and phone numbers. Two problems: (1) editorial content baked into a page template instead of the Notion pipeline; (2) the 'zh-hant' strings 欧盟 / 亚洲、中东和非洲 are SIMPLIFIED Chinese (Traditional would be 歐盟 / 亞洲、中東和非洲) — wrong script for the Traditional Chinese locale.
findings.planRef: User decision 3 (ALL editorial content flows from Notion snapshots) and decision 4 (UI strings go through translations JSON / t(), not inline records)
findings.recommendation: Move offices into a Notion database (or at minimum notion-content snapshot) with per-locale fields; have a native check of the zh-hant strings — the simplified-script strings suggest the WP extraction pulled zh-CN content into the zh-hant slot.
findings.verifierNote: Verified verbatim at src/pages/[locale]/contact/index.astro:21-61: comment 'Office data extracted from WordPress (block-3-column-special)' at line 21, the officeRegionNames record at 23-27 with the exact strings quoted, and the full hardcoded offices array (addresses, phones) at 29-61 — none of it from the Notion snapshot pipeline. The script claim checks out character-by-character: line 25 zh-hant 'Maxam Tire（欧盟）' uses simplified 欧 (Traditional: 歐盟); line 26 'Maxam Tire（亚洲、中东和非洲）' uses simplified 亚 and 东 (Traditional: 亞洲、中東和非洲). Line 24's 北美 is script-neutral, so the claim correctly targets only the eu/amea strings. Supporting context: the zh-hant industries.json data shows the same contamination (e.g. '非公路轮胎' with simplified 轮胎), consistent with zh-CN content landing in the zh-hant slot during WP extraction.

Finding stands.

findings.title: Dual root-redirect mechanisms; the Netlify _redirects rule is almost certainly inert
findings.evidence: src/pages/index.astro:7-8: `<meta http-equiv="refresh" content="0;url=/en/" /><link rel="canonical" href="/en/" />` AND public/_redirects: `/ /en/ 302`. Netlify does not apply non-forced redirects when a file exists at the path (the rule would need `302!`), so with dist/index.html present the _redirects rule is shadowed and the meta refresh is what users actually get — two mechanisms, one dead. (Behavior per Netlify's documented shadowing rules; not testable statically here.) Git history shows churn on this exact spot: commit a99527f 'better redirect'. No Accept-Language–based locale routing exists for ar-ae/zh-hant visitors.
findings.planRef: none/undocumented
findings.recommendation: Pick one mechanism: either force the Netlify rule (`/ /en/ 302!`) and delete src/pages/index.astro, or keep the static page and delete the _redirects line. Consider language-aware redirects at the edge later.
findings.verifierNote: minor — not verified

result.summary: The component layer is mid-migration and frozen there: a new "rubbermorphism" generation (17 Rubber*-prefixed components, central CSS in src/styles/components/) has fully taken over production, while the old Tailwind generation (Header, MegaNav, ProductCard, ArticleCard, FeaturedCard, IndustryCard, IndustryBadge, Hero, SpecTable) survives only as dead code or via the publicly-shipped /[locale]/design gallery. Both of the two most recent user decisions are violated at scale: Tailwind is still installed, wired into astro.config, and emitted from 19 of 39 component files plus the layout, and the banned Rubber/rubber prefix appears in 17 filenames and 235 CSS selectors — with the newest planning doc (wp-page-parity.md) actively prescribing 25+ more Rubber* components. The system is salvageable: the new generation is architecturally sound (prop-driven atoms, central CSS, one chip/button atom), and the fix is a mechanical delete (9 files) + rename (17 files + CSS classes) + Tailwind-extraction pass, not a redesign.
result.stats: 40 files audited (39 components + 1 layout): 28 production-reachable, 2 hard-dead (Header, MegaNav), 9 gallery-only (7 superseded old-gen duplicates + unused RubberEventCard + Specimen gallery infra). 17/39 files Rubber-prefixed; 235 '.rubber-' selectors across 18/27 central CSS files; ~166 Tailwind utility occurrences in 19 component files + 263 in 5 page files; 2 scoped <style> blocks (Specimen.astro, contact page); 1 empty dir (src/components/tire/); suspected 'ContentGrid' component does not exist anywhere (already removed or never built).

findings.title: Tailwind is still installed, configured, and used in 19 of 39 components plus the layout
findings.evidence: astro.config.mjs:14 `plugins: [tailwindcss()]`; src/styles/global.css:1 `@import 'tailwindcss';`; package.json:25,28 `"@tailwindcss/vite": "4.2.1", "tailwindcss": "4.2.1"`. Markup: src/layouts/BaseLayout.astro:31 `<body class="min-h-screen flex flex-col text-maxam-gray-900"`; src/components/global/RubberHeader.astro:128 `class="rubber-mega-panel__inner max-w-[var(--container-max)] mx-auto px-4 py-8 flex gap-10"`; src/components/ui/ProductsViewer.astro:59 `class="md:hidden flex items-center gap-2 mb-4 px-4 py-2 bg-maxam-blue text-maxam-white rounded-full text-sm font-semibold"`; src/components/global/Footer.astro:17 `class="grid grid-cols-1 md:grid-cols-3 gap-8"`; src/components/NotionBlocks.astro:55 `<ul class="list-disc ps-6 my-4 space-y-1">` (article body styling is 100% Tailwind, no central notion-content.css exists); src/components/ui/MaxamLogo.astro:8 docblock still says 'Tailwind classes applied to the <svg>' and emits `shrink-0` (line 20). Even the cleanest new atoms carry utilities: RubberArticleCard.astro:98, RubberBreadcrumb.astro:40, RubberProductCard.astro:115, SectionHeader.astro:41 all use `rtl:scale-x-[-1]`. Count: ~166 utility occurrences across 19 component files, +263 across 5 page files (resources/index.astro alone hand-rolls pagination/filter UI in pure Tailwind, lines 34-90).
findings.planRef: User decision #1 (NO Tailwind; semantic classes + central CSS only). CLAUDE.md:5,12 still declares 'Tailwind CSS v4' as the stack — stale per the decision override.
findings.recommendation: Plan a single extraction pass: move every utility cluster into the existing src/styles/components/*.css / pages/*.css files (the central-CSS skeleton already works — Section.astro, Grid.astro, TireGallery.astro prove the pattern), then remove `@import 'tailwindcss'` from global.css:1, the vite plugin from astro.config.mjs:14, and the three tailwind packages from package.json. Add missing central files: notion-content.css, resources.css, contact.css. The rtl:scale-x-[-1] arrow-flip needs one semantic rule (e.g. `[dir='rtl'] .icon-arrow { transform: scaleX(-1) }`).
findings.verifierNote: Every citation verified exactly: astro.config.mjs:14 'plugins: [tailwindcss()]'; global.css:1 "@import 'tailwindcss';"; package.json:25/28 pin @tailwindcss/vite and tailwindcss 4.2.1 (plus @tailwindcss/oxide-win32-x64-msvc at line 18); BaseLayout.astro:31, RubberHeader.astro:128, ProductsViewer.astro:59, Footer.astro:17, NotionBlocks.astro:55, MaxamLogo.astro:8 docblock + 'shrink-0' at line 20, and all four rtl:scale-x-[-1] sites (RubberArticleCard.astro:98, RubberBreadcrumb.astro:40, RubberProductCard.astro:115, SectionHeader.astro:41) match verbatim. Independent heuristic grep found 20 of 39 component files carrying utilities (the 19 claimed plus borderline cases) and exactly 5 page files (contact, design, product [slug], resource [slug], resources index) — resources/index.astro:34-90 hand-rolls pills/pagination in pure Tailwind as claimed. No notion-content.css/resources.css/contact.css exist (src/styles/pages has only home/products/tire.css). The ~166/+263 occurrence counts are method-dependent approximations but not overstated. CLAUDE.md:5,12 do declare the Tailwind v4 stack.

KEEP with cleanup (13): Section (exemplar), Grid (rename root class), SectionHeader (de-rubber classes), SpecViewer, SpecSizeSelector, TireGallery, ProductsViewer (strip Tailwind, use Chip atom), NotionBlocks, NotionRichText (centralize styles), Footer, LanguageSwitcher, MaxamLogo (all strip Tailwind), BaseLayout. RubberProductCard.astro:17 stale comment, RubberChip.astro:5 'Replaces the 8 ad-hoc badge / pill styles', home index.astro:58-59 '<Section background="image"', SpecViewer at products/[industry]/[slug].astro:5, and src/components/tire/ existing as an empty dir (0 items) all verified. ONE CORRECTION: "'ContentGrid' does not exist anywhere in the repo (zero grep hits)" is wrong as stated — zero hits in src/, but docs/DESIGN-SYSTEM.md:54 inventories a phantom 'src/components/ui/ContentGrid.astro' as 'Real' (file does not exist) and docs/wp-page-parity.md:40 prescribes 'ContentGrid columns={3}'. findings.title: Design gallery is a publicly shipped route in all 3 locales, contradicting its own docblock
findings.evidence:
src/components/design/Specimen.astro:7 claims 'Used only by /[locale]/_design — not shipped to production routes.' but the actual route is src/pages/[locale]/design/index.astro (no underscore prefix), a 938-line page that Astro SSG builds at /en/design/, /ar-ae/design/, /zh-hant/design/. It imports all 9 deprecated components (lines 18-27) plus 16 Rubber components (lines 28-43), contains 223 Tailwind utility occurrences and hardcoded English copy (line 108 `

Design System Gallery

`), and is the only thing keeping the dead generation referenced.
findings.planRef: Specimen.astro's own docblock; user decision #1 (utility classes) and #2 (the gallery cements Rubber naming).
findings.recommendation: Either rename the route dir to `_design` (Astro ignores underscore-prefixed routes) or gate it behind import.meta.env.DEV. Once the old generation is deleted, rewrite the gallery against the surviving atoms only. Specimen's scoped <style> (see next finding) moves to a central file if the gallery is kept.
findings.verifierNote: REFUTED — the auditor missed the build gate. design/index.astro:46-49: 'export function getStaticPaths() { if (process.env.MAXAM_SHOW_DESIGN_GALLERY !== "1") return []; ... }', and its own docblock (lines 5-7) documents 'Gated by env var: only builds when MAXAM_SHOW_DESIGN_GALLERY=1 is set.' The gate var appears nowhere in .env (0 grep hits) and the existing dist/ output contains NO design/ route in any locale (dist has en, ar-ae, zh-hant, brand, images, _astro; dist/en/design does not exist). So the page does not ship publicly, and Specimen.astro:7's 'not shipped to production routes' is substantively TRUE — only its path reference is stale ('/[locale]/_design' vs the actual non-underscored design/ dir). The evidence also overstates: 'imports all 9 deprecated components (lines 18-27)' — only 7 are imported there (Header/MegaNav are not); accurate details: 938 lines (wc -l), line 108 hardcoded English h1, gallery is the sole importer of the dead generation. Caveat: no netlify.toml exists in the repo, so a dashboard-set env var can't be ruled out from code alone — but by all repo evidence the route is dev-gated, not shipped. Residual minor items (stale _design path in Specimen docblock, hardcoded English, Tailwind in the gallery) are already covered by findings 1/2/4.

findings.title: Scoped <style> blocks in Specimen.astro and the contact page violate the central-CSS rule
findings.evidence: Only 2 scoped style blocks exist in src/, both violations: (1) src/components/design/Specimen.astro:54-65 `<style> .specimen-canvas { background-image: linear-gradient(45deg, rgba(0,0,0,0.025)...` ; (2) src/pages/[locale]/contact/index.astro:146-182 `<style> .rubber-contact-form { position: relative; background: var(--color-surface-overlay); ...}` — 37 lines of component-grade CSS (.rubber-contact-form, __top, __body) that belongs in src/styles/pages/contact.css, which does not exist (src/styles/pages/ contains only home.css, products.css, tire.css). Ironically global.css:182-183 documents the rule: 'layout / atom CSS lives in one auditable place rather than scattered across component-scoped <style> blocks.'

findings.recommendation: Create src/styles/pages/contact.css and move the .rubber-contact-form rules there (renaming per decision #2); add to global.css imports. Move .specimen-canvas into a styles/components/specimen.css or delete with the gallery decision.
findings.verifierNote: Confirmed exactly. Grep for '<style' across src/ returns precisely 2 component/page hits: Specimen.astro:54 (.specimen-canvas checker pattern, lines 54-65) and contact/index.astro:146 (block runs 146-182, 37 lines, defining .rubber-contact-form at :151 plus ::before/__top/__body — component-grade CSS). src/styles/pages/ contains only home.css, products.css, tire.css — no contact.css. global.css:181-183 states the rule verbatim ('...lives in one auditable place rather than scattered across component-scoped <style> blocks' — quoted text is at line 183). Both blocks violate user decision #1; the contact one also embeds rubber-prefixed classes (decision #2).

findings.title: Chip atom reimplemented three ways outside the RubberChip component
findings.evidence: (1) ProductsViewer.astro:95 hand-codes the atom's markup contract instead of importing it: `<button id="clear-filters" type="button" class="rubber-chip" data-variant="category" data-size="md">`; (2) ProductsViewer.astro:377-381 rebuilds chips in client JS: `chip.className = 'rubber-chip'; chip.setAttribute('data-variant', 'category'); chip.setAttribute('data-size', 'sm'); chip.innerHTML = ...×...` — bypassing RubberChip's `dismissable` API (RubberChip.astro:82-84 `rubber-chip__dismiss`); (3) resources/index.astro:34-57 hand-rolls filter pills in raw Tailwind with no chip component at all: `class="type-filter-btn px-4 py-1.5 text-sm rounded-full border border-maxam-gray-300 hover:border-maxam-blue hover:text-maxam-blue transition-colors"`. The dynamic-JS case is partially forgivable (Astro components can't render client-side) but the markup contract is now duplicated in three files that must stay in sync manually — and will all break silently when 'rubber-chip' is renamed.
findings.planRef: User decision #5: one chip atom.
findings.recommendation: Resources page filter pills must use the Chip atom (it already supports variant=filter, active, href — RubberChip.astro:28-44). For the JS-built chips, extract a single `makeChip()` helper or a <template> element rendered by the component so the class contract lives in one place. The clear-filters button (line 95) should be the component.
findings.verifierNote: All three sites verified verbatim: (1) ProductsViewer.astro:95 '<button id="clear-filters" type="button" class="rubber-chip" data-variant="category" data-size="md">' hand-codes the atom contract; (2) ProductsViewer.astro:376-385 addChip() rebuilds chips in client JS (className='rubber-chip' at 378, data-variant 379, data-size 380, innerHTML with × at 381), bypassing RubberChip's dismissable API (rubber-chip__dismiss at RubberChip.astro:83, within cited 82-84); (3) resources/index.astro:34-57 raw-Tailwind filter pills with the exact quoted class string at line 37 (plus a second industry-pill variant at 46-60 the finding didn't even count). RubberChip.astro:28-44 confirms the component already supports variant='filter', active, and href. The finding's caveat about client-side rendering being partially forgivable is fair; the three-way duplication and silent breakage on rename are real.

findings.title: Half-finished rename: component filenames and CSS class families disagree in both directions
findings.evidence: (1) SectionHeader.astro has no Rubber prefix in the filename but its docblock (line 3) says 'RubberSectionHeader — the section title row.' and it emits rubber- classes: line 31 `<header class:list={['rubber-section-header', className]}>`. (2) Inverse case: RubberIndustryCard.astro (Rubber filename) emits non-rubber classes: line 28 `class:list={['industry-card', className]}`. (3) RubberProductCard emits `'rubber-card'` (line 71) while its stylesheet is named product-card.css; RubberArticleCard emits `'rubber-article'` (line 60) in article-card.css. There is no 1:1 mapping between component name, root class, and stylesheet name, which breaks the grep-ability the central-CSS system depends on. Legacy hooks add a third naming layer: RubberProductCard.astro:73 `showFilterData && 'product-card', // legacy hook for the filter JS` and RubberArticleCard.astro:61 `'article-card', // legacy hook`.
findings.planRef: User decision #2 (no rubber classes) and #1 (central CSS auditable in one place — defeated when names don't map).
findings.recommendation: During the de-rubber rename, enforce the convention component X.astro → root class .x → styles/components/x.css. Decide whether the 'product-card'/'article-card' filter-JS hooks become the real root classes (they are the post-rename names anyway) and drop the dual-class hack.
findings.verifierNote: All six citations verbatim: SectionHeader.astro:3 docblock 'RubberSectionHeader — the section title row.' with no Rubber filename, emitting 'rubber-section-header' at :31 (and __lead/__heading/__arrow throughout); RubberIndustryCard.astro:28 emits non-rubber 'industry-card' despite the Rubber filename; RubberProductCard.astro:71 'rubber-card' styled by product-card.css; RubberArticleCard.astro:60 'rubber-article' styled by article-card.css; legacy dual-class hooks at RubberProductCard.astro:73 (showFilterData && 'product-card', // legacy hook for the filter JS) and RubberArticleCard.astro:61 ('article-card', // legacy hook for the resources filter JS). Stylesheet listing confirms the name mismatches (product-card.css, article-card.css, industry-card.css, section-header.css). The three-layer naming (filename vs root class vs stylesheet vs legacy JS hook) is accurately characterized.

findings.verifierNote: minor — not verified

findings.title: Grid component's root class '.grid' collides with Tailwind's grid utility
findings.evidence: styles/components/grid.css:20-23 defines `.grid { display: grid; width: 100%; }` for the Grid component (Grid.astro:22 `class:list={['grid', ...]`), while pages simultaneously use Tailwind's `.grid` utility for ad-hoc layouts: resources/index.astro:69 `<div id="article-grid" class="grid grid-cols-1 sm:grid-cols-2 lg:grid-cols-3 gap-6">`, design/index.astro:123 `class="grid grid-cols-1 md:grid-cols-2 gap-6"`. Because grid.css is un-layered author CSS and Tailwind v4 utilities live in a cascade layer, the component's `width: 100%` rule also applies to every Tailwind grid div — two systems sharing one class name with different intents. Currently mostly benign (both set display:grid) but it makes the eventual Tailwind removal hazardous: pages relying on utility `.grid` will silently inherit only the component rule.
findings.planRef: User decision #1/#5 (one layout system).
findings.recommendation: Rename the component root class (e.g. .col-grid or .layout-grid) during the rename pass, and convert the two raw-Tailwind grid usages in resources/design pages to the Grid component.
findings.verifierNote: minor — not verified

findings.title: Second styled-select implementation in SpecSizeSelector instead of the Select atom

result.domain: CSS & design system implementation (src/styles/**, Tailwind usage in src/**/*.astro)
result.summary: The semantic-CSS migration is roughly half real: a coherent, well-crafted "Rubbermorphism" component CSS layer exists (29 files, all imported, tokens consumed consistently), but it is structurally hostage to Tailwind — every design token lives inside a Tailwind `@theme` block, and 1,858+ utility-class occurrences remain across 26 of 49 .astro files, including the live header, footer, NotionBlocks, resources, and contact pages, with JS toggling Tailwind classes at runtime. Decision #2 (no rubber prefix) is violated by 104 unique `.rubber-*` selectors across 18 of 26 component stylesheets plus a scoped `<style>` block. The design language that actually shipped is neither the WP token system in IMPLEMENTATION-PLAN.md (zero of its values appear in src/styles) nor documented anywhere — "rubbermorphism" is only name-dropped in wp-page-parity.md without a token spec. The system is salvageable: dead CSS is small (4 rules, 14 dead tokens), there are no orphan files, and the rubber rename plus a `@theme`→`:root` token move are mechanical; the long tail is converting the ~8 still-Tailwind live surfaces.
result.stats: 30 CSS files in src/styles (1 global + 26 component + 3 page; all 29 imported by global.css — 0 orphans). Tailwind: 1,858+ utility occurrences in 26/49 .astro files (lower bound — frontmatter-composed strings like IndustryBadge.astro escaped the scan); 0-utility files: 19 Rubber*/new components + products pages. 104 unique .rubber-* selectors in 18/26 component CSS files (+3 in contact page <style>). Dead: 4 CSS rules, 14 @theme tokens (10 industry colors, 2 spacing, 2 parallax). 2 scoped <style> blocks. Duplication: chamfer ::before block x15 in 12 files; rgba(31,29,26,…) literal x50; #c83232 x6 in 4 files; input/select/textarea ~90% identical. 10 distinct px breakpoints across stylesheets.

findings.title: Tailwind is load-bearing for the entire site; removing `@import 'tailwindcss'` would break every page
findings.evidence: src/styles/global.css:1 `@import 'tailwindcss';` and global.css:8 `@theme {` — ALL ~50 design tokens (--color-*, --shadow-*, --radius-*, --z-*, --duration-*, --ease-*, --container-max) are declared inside `@theme`, a Tailwind-only at-rule. Without Tailwind the block is an unknown at-rule, every `var(--color-surface-raised)` etc. in all 29 semantic CSS files resolves to nothing, and the whole Rubbermorphism layer loses color/shadow/motion. Markup: 1,858+ utility occurrences in 26 .astro files. Live-page dependence: src/layouts/BaseLayout.astro:31 `<body class="min-h-screen flex flex-col text-maxam-gray-900"...>` and :33 `<main class="flex-1">`; RubberHeader.astro (106 occurrences, e.g. :68 `<nav class="hidden md:flex items-center gap-1.5">`); Footer.astro (21); NotionBlocks.astro (81 — renders all Notion content); resources/index.astro (81); contact/index.astro (35); resources/[slug].astro (30); ProductsViewer.astro (39). Runtime JS toggles Tailwind classes: RubberHeader.astro:300/316/350 `panels.forEach((p) => p.classList.add('hidden'))`; ProductsViewer.astro:295/395/402; resources/index.astro:223-225 `btn.classList.toggle('bg-maxam-blue', isActive)`. Also global.css:185-219 places `@import './components/*.css'` AFTER style rules (body at :145) — spec-invalid in plain CSS, currently tolerated only because the Tailwind/Vite pipeline inlines them (not verified at build level, but the ordering violation is in the source).
findings.planRef: User decision #1 (NO Tailwind; semantic classes + central CSS only)
findings.recommendation: Two-step decouple: (1) move the entire token block from `@theme` to `:root` (only --color-maxam-* are ever consumed as generated utilities; everything else is plain var() consumption) and hoist component/page @imports above all rules; (2) convert the 8 live Tailwind surfaces (BaseLayout body, RubberHeader chrome wrappers, Footer, NotionBlocks, resources index/detail, contact, ProductsViewer overlay) to semantic classes, replacing JS-toggled `hidden`/`flex`/`bg-maxam-blue` with data-attribute state selectors (pattern already proven by `#filter-sidebar[data-open]` in global.css:159-167). Only then delete line 1 and the tailwind deps.
findings.verifierNote: Re-checked every citation. global.css:1 `@import 'tailwindcss';` and :8 `@theme {` exact; all cited token namespaces (--color-*, --shadow-*, --radius-*, --z-*, --duration-*, --ease-*, --container-max) are declared inside @theme lines 8-117 (only --page-gutter lives in :root at 128-136), so dropping Tailwind orphans every var() in the semantic CSS. BaseLayout.astro:31 and :33 quotes are verbatim; RubberHeader.astro:68 verbatim; JS toggles confirmed at RubberHeader.astro:300/316/350, ProductsViewer.astro:295/395/402 (plus :411), resources/index.astro:223-225 (plus 148-155 and 229-231 — evidence is understated, not overstated). Independent token count: 1,760 Tailwind-like utility tokens in exactly 26 .astro files (auditor: 1,858+ — within counting-method noise; per-file counts match within a few: design 926 vs 961, RubberHeader 96 vs 106, Footer 20 vs 21, NotionBlocks 77 vs 81, contact 35 vs 35). @import-after-rules confirmed: component/page @imports at global.css:185-219 follow the body rule at :145, spec-invalid in plain CSS and currently absorbed by the Vite/Tailwind pipeline. tailwindcss 4.2.1 + @tailwindcss/vite confirmed in package.json. No refutation found.

findings.title: 104 `.rubber-*` class selectors across 18 of 26 component stylesheets violate the no-rubber-prefix decision
findings.evidence: Unique .rubber-* selectors per file: header.css 13 (excluding 2 comment-only mentions at :209), product-card.css 13, article-card.css 11, office-card.css 10, featured-card.css 8, document-card.css 8, event-card.css 7, testimonial-card.css 6, section-header.css 6, breadcrumb.css 5, field.css 5, footer.css 4, chip.css 3, button.css 1 (.rubber-btn), input.css 1, select.css 1, textarea.css 1, plate.css 1 = 104 unique selectors. Plus 3 more defined OUTSIDE styles/ in a scoped block: src/pages/[locale]/contact/index.astro:151 `.rubber-contact-form`, :170 `__top`, :176 `__body`. Non-compliant naming coexists with compliant files in the same system: industry-card.css uses `.industry-card`, filter-sidebar.css `.filter-sidebar`, tire-gallery.css `.tire-gallery`, spec-viewer.css `.spec-viewer` — two naming conventions in one directory.

The 8 already-semantic files (section, grid, industry-card, filter-sidebar, tire-gallery, spec-viewer, spec-selector, spec-table) define the target convention.

Contact scoped block confirmed at contact/index.astro:151 (.rubber-contact-form), :170 (__top), :176 (__body). Compliant counter-examples confirmed (industry-card.css, filter-sidebar.css, tire-gallery.css, spec-viewer.css, plus section/grid/spec-selector/spec-table at zero rubber selectors). findings.title: Shipped design system is an undocumented invention; the documented WP token system was never implemented
findings.evidence: docs/IMPLEMENTATION-PLAN.md:85-97 specifies Sky Blue #e5f1f9, Dark Blue #1c2858, Green #009042, Orange #f79425, Red #9e0b0f, Yellow #dcb127, 'Typography: Helvetica Neue family. Container: 1170px. Pill buttons: 28px radius.' — grep of src/ finds NONE of these in any stylesheet (only in Notion data snapshots and mock-tires.ts as industry data colors). The term 'rubbermorphism' appears in NO spec: docs/wp-page-parity.md:5/381/399/416 references it as established vocabulary ('we're using rubbermorphism, not the WP theme's design language') but never defines a single token; DESIGN-SYSTEM.md (2026-06-07) documents the OLD palette and says 'No shared Button component' (:10) when button.css + RubberButton now exist. Only #0077c8 (--color-maxam-blue) survives from the WP system.

Either way DESIGN-SYSTEM.md sections 2-4 need a rewrite — they inventory a component landscape that no longer exists.
findings.verifierNote: docs/IMPLEMENTATION-PLAN.md:85-97 verified verbatim (token table #e5f1f9/#1c2858/#0077c8/#009042/#f79425/#9e0b0f/#dcb127; line 97 'Typography: Helvetica Neue family. Container: 1170px. Pill buttons: 28px radius.'). Grep of src/ confirms none of those hexes, no 'Helvetica', no '1170' in any .css/.astro — the hexes appear only in src/data/mock-tires.ts and src/data/notion-content/*.json as industry data. global.css:50 'Rubbermorphism: physical layering system' comment, 1280px container at :48, system-ui stack at :146 all confirmed. wp-page-parity.md:5/381/399/416 use 'rubbermorphism' as established vocabulary without defining tokens (also 'rubbermorphic' at 225/242/380); 'rubbermorph' appears nowhere in DESIGN-SYSTEM.md or IMPLEMENTATION-PLAN.md. DESIGN-SYSTEM.md header confirms 'Audit captured 2026-06-07' and it documents the old Tailwind-utility landscape (ring-maxam-blue etc.). One off-by-one: 'No shared Button component' is at DESIGN-SYSTEM.md:9, not :10 — button.css and RubberButton.astro do now exist. '#0077c8 only survivor' holds for stylesheets.

findings.title: Two complete design systems ship in parallel, kept alive by the ungated design gallery and two fully orphaned components
findings.evidence: Legacy Tailwind components ProductCard, ArticleCard, FeaturedCard, IndustryCard, IndustryBadge, Hero, SpecTable, Section/SectionHeader-era styling are imported ONLY by src/pages/[locale]/design/index.astro:18-27 (961 utility occurrences — half the site total), which has no env gating (no import.meta.env in the file, contradicting DESIGN-SYSTEM.md:264 'gated by env var so it doesn't ship to production') and builds for all 3 locales. Header.astro (127 utilities) and MegaNav.astro (93) are imported by NO page at all — BaseLayout.astro:3 uses RubberHeader — yet Tailwind scans them and emits their utilities into the sitewide stylesheet. spec-table.css is imported globally (global.css:200) though its consumer SpecTable.astro is gallery-only; the live product page uses SpecViewer + spec-viewer.css (products/[industry]/[slug].astro:5). FeaturedCard.astro:8 still pulls IndustryBadge — a second badge implementation alongside RubberChip.
findings.planRef: User decision #5 (one chip atom, one button atom, one card language); DESIGN-SYSTEM.md section 7
findings.recommendation: Delete Header.astro and MegaNav.astro outright (zero importers). Either gate /[locale]/design behind an env check or rebuild it on the new atoms; then delete ProductCard/ArticleCard/FeaturedCard/IndustryCard/IndustryBadge/Hero/SpecTable + spec-table.css. This alone removes ~1,300 of the 1,858 utility occurrences.
findings.verifierNote: Core finding holds but a central evidence claim is REFUTED: the gallery IS env-gated. design/index.astro:46-49 reads `if (process.env.MAXAM_SHOW_DESIGN_GALLERY !== '1') return [];` in getStaticPaths — the auditor grepped only for import.meta.env and missed the process.env gate. The var is set nowhere (not in .env/.env.example, no netlify.toml exists), and dist/en/ contains no design/ output, so it does NOT 'build for all 3 locales' and does NOT contradict DESIGN-SYSTEM.md:263-264 — that proposal is implemented. Second correction: the cited import range :18-27 includes Section(:24)/SectionHeader(:25)/Grid(:26), which are CURRENT-system pure-markup components (semantic classes in section.css/grid.css/section-header.css) imported by live pages ([locale]/index.astro:3-5, products/[industry]/index.astro:4-6) — not legacy. What survives, verified: Header.astro and MegaNav.astro have zero page importers (only Header imports MegaNav; BaseLayout.astro:3 uses RubberHeader); ProductCard/ArticleCard/IndustryCard/Hero/SpecTable are gallery-only imports; FeaturedCard.astro:8 imports IndustryBadge (FeaturedCard is also imported by the orphaned Header/MegaNav, not gallery-only strictly); spec-table.css is globally imported at global.css:200 while its consumer is gallery-only and the live product page uses SpecViewer ([industry]/[slug].astro:5). Crucially the 'kept alive' claim is true DESPITE the gate: Tailwind 4.2.1 auto source detection scans the un-routed sources — gallery-only utilities bg-green-100/bg-yellow-100/bg-orange-100 (Specimen status badges) are demonstrably present in shipped dist/_astro/BaseLayout.DRdTpaBJ.css. Remediation note: 'gate behind an env check' is already done; the fix is deletion or @source exclusion.

findings.title: Semantic `.grid` class collides with Tailwind's `grid` utility
