result.summary: The Notion→snapshot→data.ts→pages architecture is real and well-built: pages/components touch only src/lib/data.ts, sync-from-notion.ts produces every collection snapshot, and the block-sidecar design is sound. But the pipeline's operational substrate is broken: the ENTIRE scripts/ directory (including the live sync script that package.json invokes, the load-bearing build-product-specs.ts, and the irreplaceable notion-ids.json config) is gitignored, and five snapshot files that data.ts statically imports (pages, page-promos, product-specs x3) have never been committed — a fresh clone can neither build nor sync. The known product-specs open loop is confirmed and worse than stated: specs come from a frozen May 4 WP export, the Notion copies of the spec tables were lossily flattened at import time, so Notion can never become the spec source without a new structured import. Roughly half of the 50 scripts are finished migration artifacts or probes that should be archived or deleted; mock-tires.ts is confirmed dead.
result.stats: 50 scripts in scripts/: 2 live-pipeline (only sync-from-notion.ts wired via npm sync/sync:fast; build-product-specs.ts load-bearing but unwired), 3 shared helper libs (notion-client, db, html-to-notion), 28 one-time migration (done), 3 WP→Notion transition tools, 13 probe/debug junk candidates, 1 dead (generate-mock-data). scripts/output/: 48 files / 15MB, only 4 still load-bearing. Snapshots: 16 top-level files + 1,328 block sidecars; 5 snapshot files untracked; 0 files of scripts/ in git. Last commit 2026-05-04; last sync 2026-06-10 ran --without-blocks.

findings.title: Entire live sync pipeline is gitignored — a fresh clone cannot run npm run sync at all
findings.evidence: .gitignore:35-36 — "# WP migration scripts and extracted data\nscripts/" ignores the WHOLE directory. `git ls-files scripts/` returns ZERO files. Yet package.json:13-14 wires the live pipeline into that ignored directory: "sync": "tsx scripts/sync-from-notion.ts", "sync:fast": "tsx scripts/sync-from-notion.ts --without-blocks". scripts/sync-from-notion.ts, scripts/build-product-specs.ts, and scripts/output/notion-ids.json (required by src/lib/notion/client.ts:60 `join(process.cwd(), 'scripts', 'output', 'notion-ids.json')`) exist only on this one machine. The blanket ignore was written for WP migration artifacts but now swallows recurring-pipeline code.
findings.planRef: User decision 3 (sync-from-notion.ts is THE live pipeline); sync-from-notion.ts:5-7 claims the build is reproducible from the repo
findings.recommendation: Replace the blanket `scripts/` ignore with `scripts/output/` (plus explicit exceptions), commit sync-from-notion.ts and build-product-specs.ts, and move notion-ids.json out of scripts/output/ into a tracked config location (the IDs are not secrets — the token in .env is). Decide explicitly which migration scripts to track as archive.
findings.verifierNote: Re-checked everything. Citation correction: the ignore rule is .gitignore:32-33 ('# WP migration scripts and extracted data' / 'scripts/'), not :35-36. All substance holds: `git ls-files scripts/` returns 0 files (50 .ts scripts plus scripts/output/ are untracked); package.json:13-14 is exactly '"sync": "tsx scripts/sync-from-notion.ts"' / '"sync:fast": "tsx scripts/sync-from-notion.ts --without-blocks"'; src/lib/notion/client.ts:60 is exactly `join(process.cwd(), 'scripts', 'output', 'notion-ids.json')` inside getNotionIds(); sync-from-notion.ts:6-7 claims 'Snapshot files are committed to the repo so the Astro build is fully offline'. A remote exists (github.com/tranzillo/maxam-tires) but since scripts/ is fully untracked, none of the pipeline code can be on it — 'exists only on this one machine' is a sound inference for the repo, though I cannot verify absence of out-of-band copies.

findings.title: data.ts statically imports 5 snapshot files that were never committed — fresh clone fails `astro build`
findings.evidence: src/lib/data.ts:31-35 — `import pagesJson from '../data/notion-content/pages.json'; import pagePromosJson from '../data/notion-content/page-promos.json'; import productSpecsEn from '../data/notion-content/product-specs.en.json';` (+ ar-ae, zh-hant). `git status --short` shows all five as untracked: `?? src/data/notion-content/page-promos.json`, `?? pages.json`, `?? product-specs.{ar-ae,en,zh-hant}.json` (not gitignored — simply never added; `git check-ignore` returns nothing). This directly contradicts sync-from-notion.ts:5-7: "Snapshot files are committed to the repo so the Astro build is fully offline". Any CI/Netlify build from a clean checkout fails module resolution. No netlify.toml exists, so I cannot verify how production deploys are configured — if they build from git, deploys are currently broken; if from this machine via CLI, the project has a bus factor of one.
findings.planRef: sync-from-notion.ts:5-7 (committed-snapshot contract); user decision 3
findings.recommendation: git add the five files immediately. Add a CI guard (or a build-time assertion) that every file data.ts imports is tracked. Verify the Netlify build source.
findings.verifierNote: Verified exactly. src/lib/data.ts:31-35 contains the five static imports (pages.json, page-promos.json, product-specs.{en,ar-ae,zh-hant}.json); `git status --short` shows all five as `??` untracked; `git check-ignore` on all five exits 1 (not ignored — never added); sync-from-notion.ts:6-7 carries the committed-snapshot contract (auditor cited :5-7, quote is at 6-7). Confirmed no netlify.toml, no .netlify/ dir, and no netlify mention in astro.config — the deploy-source uncertainty is honestly stated, not asserted. Last commit f00fd5e is 2026-05-04, so the files have been missing from every commit. A clean checkout cannot resolve these JSON module imports; the build-failure claim is sound.

findings.title: Product specs do NOT flow from Notion — frozen WP export is the source, and the Notion copies are too lossy to ever parse (the known open loop, precisely mapped)
findings.evidence: Chain: (1) scripts/build-product-specs.ts:42 `const SRC_DIR = join(process.cwd(), 'scripts', 'output')` and :285 `join(SRC_DIR, `tires-${locale}.json`)` — reads the WP export (file dates May 4), writes product-specs.{locale}.json. (2) src/lib/data.ts:33-35 imports those files; :610-612 getProductSpecs serves them. (3) src/pages/[locale]/products/[industry]/[slug].astro:85 `const hasStructuredSpecs = !!specs;` and :96-97 — when structured specs exist the page DISCARDS the Notion spec-table blocks (`if (!hasStructuredSpecs) specsBlocks.push(b)`), so editing the spec table in Notion changes NOTHING on the site. Worse: products created in Notion after May 4 can never get structured specs (frozen source), and the loop cannot be closed by parsing Notion blocks, because the import was lossy — scripts/import-products-to-notion.ts:102-110 clean() does `.replace(/\s+/g, ' ')`, destroying the newline-separated imperial/metric cells that build-product-specs.ts depends on (mergeCellUnits splits on '\n', :100; detectLayout needs distinct unit rows, :114-122), and literal '#colspan#' marker headers were written into Notion table cells (specTableBlock :153-155 maps clean over headers without stripping them).
findings.planRef: User decision 3: ALL editorial content flows from Notion; scripts/output/ is never a live data source
findings.recommendation: Closing the loop requires: (1) a structured 'Product Specs' Notion database (one row per product variant, or per product with structured properties — NOT free-form table blocks); (2) a one-time import script seeded from the already-normalized product-specs.{locale}.json (the cleanest surviving form of the data); (3) a fetchProductSpecs() in src/lib/notion/fetchers.ts and a sync step in sync-from-notion.ts writing product-specs.{locale}.json; (4) retire build-product-specs.ts and the tires-*.json dependency; (5) optionally delete the degraded table blocks from Notion product pages to remove the misleading edit surface.
findings.verifierNote: Every citation verified exactly: build-product-specs.ts:42 SRC_DIR=scripts/output, :285 `join(SRC_DIR, \`tires-${locale}.json\`)` (tires-*.json mtimes May 4); data.ts:33-35 imports + :610-612 getProductSpecs; [slug].astro:85 `const hasStructuredSpecs = !!specs;` and :96-97 `if (!hasStructuredSpecs) specsBlocks.push(b)` — Notion spec-table edits are indeed discarded whenever structured specs exist; import-products-to-notion.ts:102-110 clean() does `.replace(/\s+/g, ' ')` and specTableBlock:153-155 maps clean over columns/rows without stripping '#colspan#'; mergeCellUnits splits on '\n' (:99-100, used at :252 in normalizeSingle); detectLayout :114-122 as described. Data check: tires-en.json has 117 spec tables, 36 with #colspan# headers, 11 with newline cells. One overstatement to flag: 'too lossy to EVER parse' is too absolute — #colspan# markers and the separate unit rows actually SURVIVE the Notion import (the markers are pollution, not loss), so paired/colspan layouts are in principle re-parseable from Notion; the genuinely destroyed information is the intra-cell '\n' in single-layout cells (11/117 en tables). The core finding (frozen May-4 source, post-May-4 Notion products can never get structured specs, Notion edit surface is dead) is fully confirmed.

findings.title: build-product-specs.ts is mislabeled 'one-time migration' but is a required recurring producer, wired into nothing
findings.evidence: scripts/build-product-specs.ts:2-3 header: "one-time migration: normalize each product's spec table..." and :9-10 "once these clean specs are pushed back into Notion... this script is retired" — but the push-back never happened, its outputs are statically imported by src/lib/data.ts:33-35 (build fails without them), and it is absent from package.json scripts (only dev/build/preview/astro/sync/sync:fast exist). Its mtime (Jun 10 11:58) shows it was hand-edited and re-run today — active, manual, undocumented operation. A maintainer running `npm run sync` gets fresh products.json but spec tables silently keyed to slugs from the May 4 WP export.
findings.planRef: Known open loop in user decision 3
findings.recommendation: Until the Notion specs DB exists: wire it as `npm run sync` post-step (or `sync` = sync-from-notion && build-product-specs), commit its outputs, and fix the header comment to state it is live and load-bearing.
findings.verifierNote: Verified exactly. Header lines 2-3 say 'one-time migration: normalize each product's spec table' and lines 8-10 say 'once these clean specs are pushed back into Notion, the live sync path will read them from there instead and this script is retired' (auditor cited :9-10; spans 8-10). Outputs statically imported at data.ts:33-35 (build fails without them, per F2). package.json scripts block contains only dev/build/preview/astro/sync/sync:fast — confirmed. Recency confirmed and strengthened: script mtime Jun 10 11:58, and its three outputs product-specs.{en,ar-ae,zh-hant}.json all have mtime Jun 10 11:59 — edited and re-run today, manually, outside any npm script.

findings.title: notion-ids.json is an untracked, machine-local single point of failure; losing it and re-running setup scripts would mint duplicate empty databases
findings.evidence: scripts/output/notion-ids.json (1,260 bytes, 22 DB/data-source UUIDs) is inside the gitignored scripts/ tree — not in git (`git ls-files scripts/output/` is empty). src/lib/notion/client.ts:58-63 hard-requires it for every sync (`readFileSync(join(process.cwd(),'scripts','output','notion-ids.json'))`), and sync-from-notion.ts:115-119 throws if pagesDataSourceId is missing. Recovery path is a trap: create-notion-products-db.ts:2-3 "We create the database if missing" — 'missing' is judged by this file, so re-running the create-* scripts after losing it creates brand-new EMPTY databases, orphaning 351 products / 972 articles / 390 documents of populated content. .env.example:6-7 even documents this file as the ID source.
findings.planRef: User decision 3 (sync must be reliably runnable); none/undocumented for the recovery hazard
findings.recommendation: Move notion-ids.json to a tracked path (e.g. src/lib/notion/notion-ids.json or config/); make create-* scripts query Notion for existing databases by title before creating; keep only NOTION_TOKEN in .env.
findings.verifierNote: Confirmed with two corrections: (1) the file holds 20 UUIDs (10 dbId + 10 dataSourceId pairs), not 22 — file size 1,260 bytes is exact; (2) the .env.example reference is at lines 5-6, not 6-7 ('the runtime fetchers read database IDs from scripts/output/notion-ids.json'). The recovery trap is verified in code, not just the comment: create-notion-products-db.ts:52-60 reads ids from IDS_FILE (`if (existsSync(IDS_FILE))`, else `ids = {}`) and :60 `if (!dbId)` immediately creates a brand-new database under NOTION_PARENT_PAGE_ID — losing the file mints empty duplicates exactly as claimed. client.ts:58-63 confirmed (note: it reads lazily so plain `astro build` does not need the file — the finding correctly scopes the hard requirement to sync; sync-from-notion.ts throws at :115-120 and :131-136). Orphan counts 351/972/390 confirmed from _manifest.json.

findings.title: import-pages-to-notion.ts is non-idempotent by design — re-run duplicates every Pages/Page Promos row
findings.evidence: scripts/import-pages-to-notion.ts:14-16 (own header): "Idempotency: re-running creates duplicate rows (Notion has no natural key here), so this is meant to run ONCE. If you need to re-run, clear the two databases first." Confirmed in code: the loop at :118-132 calls notion.pages.create unconditionally with no Slug+Language existence check, unlike incremental-sync.ts which diffs by WP ID first. Duplicates would then poison the snapshot: src/lib/data.ts:545-546 and :562 use `pages.find(...)` — first match wins, so which duplicate's content ships becomes ordering-dependent. The file was touched Jun 10 11:50, so it is still being run, not archived.

findings.recommendation: Add a pre-flight query by (Slug, Language) that switches to update-or-skip, or make the script refuse to run when the target data sources are non-empty. Alternatively archive it and declare clear-notion-data-source.ts + re-import as the only re-seed path, in a tracked runbook.
findings.verifierNote: Verified exactly. Header lines 14-16 quote is verbatim ('Idempotency: re-running creates duplicate rows... clear the two databases first'). The Pages loop at :118-132 calls notion.pages.create (:123) unconditionally — the only guard is --dry-run; no Slug+Language existence query (promos loop :136-150 likewise). Contrast confirmed: incremental-sync.ts:7 'query Notion for the set of existing WP IDs' and :86 `if (existing.has(t.wpId))` skips. Duplicate-poisoning path confirmed: data.ts:546 and :562 both use `pages.find(...)` — first match wins. Recency confirmed and strengthened: script mtime Jun 10 11:50, and its outputs notion-pages-map.json / notion-page-promos-map.json (written at :153-154) have mtime Jun 10 11:51 — it actually RAN today, not merely edited.

findings.evidence: sync-from-notion.ts:103/220 fetches events and writes event-* block sidecars, but data.ts has no getEventBlocks — readBlocks('event', …) (data.ts:49) is unreachable. data.ts:478-479 docstring: "Everything that should appear on the Resource Center page: articles (blog + news + events)" — yet getAllResources (:485-493) merges only `articles` and `documents`; the events array is untouched. data.ts:467 builds `href: `/${locale}/resources/${e.slug}`` but src/pages/[locale]/resources/[slug].astro:11-21 getStaticPaths enumerates getAllArticles only, so those URLs 404. findings.title: Snapshot state drift: 5 weeks of uncommitted content, and the latest sync skipped block bodies, leaving metadata (Jun 10) and bodies (May 4) from different eras
findings.evidence: Last commit f00fd5e "content resync" is 2026-05-04 09:10; `git status` shows every snapshot modified and uncommitted since. src/data/notion-content/_manifest.json: {"syncedAt":"2026-06-10T15:56:08.524Z","withBlocks":false,...} — today's sync was sync:fast, so the 1,328 block sidecars all date from May 4 while row metadata is from today. Any product/article body edited or created in Notion since May 4 renders stale or empty (readBlocks returns [] for missing files, data.ts:51). Nothing records per-row block freshness; the manifest has only the single global withBlocks flag.
findings.planRef: sync-from-notion.ts:3-7 contract (sync then commit, build offline)
findings.recommendation: Adopt a sync→specs→commit ritual or CI job; have sync warn when a row's last_edited_time in Notion is newer than its sidecar file; consider recording per-type blocksSyncedAt in the manifest.
findings.verifierNote: Verified exactly. `git log`: f00fd5e 'content resync' 2026-05-04 09:10:24. `git status` shows every notion-content snapshot modified-uncommitted. _manifest.json reads {"syncedAt":"2026-06-10T15:56:08.524Z","withBlocks":false} with no per-type block freshness — the single global flag is all there is. Sidecar count is exactly 1,328 files; `find -newermt 2026-05-05` returns 0, newest files are May 4 09:05 — the metadata/bodies era split is precisely as described. data.ts:51 `if (!existsSync(file)) return [];` confirms new rows render with empty bodies rather than erroring.

findings.title: README and CLAUDE.md describe a pipeline that no longer exists; a new developer cannot discover how content flows
findings.evidence: README.md lists only dev/build/preview in its Commands table — `npm run sync`, NOTION_TOKEN, snapshots, and build-product-specs are never mentioned; it still headlines "Tailwind CSS v4 — Utility-first CSS". CLAUDE.md ("Current State as of 2026-03-23") claims "Mock data: src/data/mock-tires.ts", next step "1. Create Notion databases" (they exist and hold 351 products), "MySQL port 10017" while scripts/db.ts:6 says `port: 10023`, and routes under `[locale]/tires/` that are now `[locale]/products/`. Compounding it, .gitignore:29-33 ignores CLAUDE.md and docs/ themselves, so the project's contract documents are also untracked and unversioned.
findings.planRef: CLAUDE.md, README.md (the stated contract)
findings.recommendation: Rewrite README's setup section around the real flow (env → notion-ids → sync → build); update or retire CLAUDE.md's Current State; stop gitignoring the contract docs.
findings.verifierNote: Verified with one citation correction. README.md: Commands table (lines 20-25) lists only install/dev/build/preview; no mention of sync, NOTION_TOKEN, snapshots, or build-product-specs anywhere; line 8 headlines 'Tailwind CSS v4 — Utility-first CSS'. findings.title: Two parallel Notion clients with two different .env conventions
findings.evidence: scripts/notion-client.ts:5 uses dotenv (`dotenv.config({ path: join(import.meta.dirname, '..', '.env') })`) and hard-requires BOTH NOTION_TOKEN and NOTION_PARENT_PAGE_ID (:10-11 throws). src/lib/notion/client.ts:14-28 ships a second hand-rolled loadEnv() parser and requires only NOTION_TOKEN (:31-33). Migration scripts use the former, the live sync the latter; behavior differs (quote stripping, throw conditions), and the hand-rolled parser exists despite dotenv already being a devDependency (package.json:26).

findings.title: Sync never deletes orphaned block sidecar files
findings.evidence: sync-from-notion.ts splitBlocks (:59-67) only writes `${type}-${language}-${slug}.json`; nothing removes sidecars for rows that were deleted or re-slugged in Notion. incremental-sync.ts header makes it policy: "What this script does NOT do: ... Delete sidecar block files for archived rows (left in place; harmless)". Not harmless forever: 1,328 tracked files accumulate, and data.ts readBlocks (:49-57) will happily serve a stale orphan if a slug is ever reused.
findings.planRef: none/undocumented
findings.recommendation: At the end of a full-blocks sync, diff the blocks/ directory against the expected filename set and delete (or at least report) orphans.
findings.verifierNote: minor — not verified

result.domain: Hardcoded content that bypasses the Notion pipeline
result.summary: The home page is genuinely Notion-driven (Pages + Page Promos snapshots), but it is the ONLY page that is: pages.json contains exactly one slug ('home' x3 locales), so the contact page ships a fully hardcoded office dataset with inline tri-locale translations in code, and the products/resources heroes are not editable at all. Two hardcoded-slug assumptions ('en' industry slugs in getStaticPaths, 'rubber-tracks' filter on home) collide with Notion's locale-suffixed slugs and structurally break ar-ae/zh-hant navigation and home-page parity. Several CTAs (including one stored in Notion itself, /sustainability) point at routes that do not exist, and the 'event' resource type is wired through nav/filters but can never be produced by the pipeline. The system is salvageable — the pipeline plumbing (pageText/getPagePromos) works — but it needs Pages records for the remaining routes, a featured flag in Notion, and removal of en-slug matching.
result.stats: 9 live routes + 40 components swept; pages.json covers 1 of ~4 content pages ('home' only); public/images: 5 files, all 5 referenced by Notion snapshot values AND duplicated as hardcoded markup/fallbacks; ~900 product/article/industry images hotlink legacy maxamtire.com; 121 t() keys x3 locales (complete), of which ~60 category.* keys are dead duplicates of Notion taxonomy names; ~25 hardcoded English aria-label/alt strings; 1 hardcoded featured-slug list; 2 dead-end CTAs (/testimonials, /sustainability); 1 office dataset (3 regions, 7 addresses, 5 phones) hardcoded in a page frontmatter

findings.title: Industry hub routes generated from hardcoded 'en' slugs while Notion slugs are locale-suffixed — ar-ae/zh-hant industry navigation is broken
findings.evidence: c:/Users/kappa/Documents/Projects/maxam-tires/src/pages/[locale]/products/[industry]/index.astro:19-26 — `const industries = getAllIndustries('en'); ... for (const locale of locales) { for (const industry of industries) { paths.push({ params: { locale, industry: industry.slug } }); } }`. Snapshot slugs differ per locale (src/data/notion-content/industries.json): en has 'rubber-tracks', 'tbr-tires', 'agricultural-tires'; ar-ae has 'rubber-tracks-ar-ae', 'tbr-ar-ae'; zh-hant has 'rubber-tracks-zh-hant', 'tbr-tires-zh-hant', 'agricultural-tractor-tires'. So /zh-hant/products/agricultural-tires is built but `getIndustryBySlug(locale, industrySlug)` fails → redirect (line 32-34), while the mega nav links to the locale slug that was never built: RubberHeader.astro:143 `href={localePath(locale, `/products/${ind.slug}/`)}` → /zh-hant/products/agricultural-tractor-tires/ → 404. Product detail paths use locale slugs ([slug].astro:22 `industry: tire.industries[0].slug`), so zh-hant tire pages exist under industry segments whose hub page 404s (breadcrumb at [slug].astro:115 also dead).
findings.planRef: User decision 6 (locale layouts/structure identical across locales; no English-matching logic); CLAUDE.md/IMPLEMENTATION-PLAN 3-locale parity
findings.recommendation: Either normalize slugs to be locale-stable at sync time (strip the '-ar-ae'/'-zh-hant' suffixes Notion/WPML produced, keyed by trid), or generate [industry] paths per-locale from that locale's own industries. Slug normalization is the better fix — it also repairs LanguageSwitcher prefix-swapping and breadcrumbs.
findings.verifierNote: Verified every link in the chain: [industry]/index.astro:19 `getAllIndustries('en')` with locale x en-slug cross-product (19-26) and redirect on miss (32-34); industries.json per-locale slugs match the quote exactly (en: rubber-tracks/tbr-tires/agricultural-tires; ar-ae: rubber-tracks-ar-ae/tbr-ar-ae; zh-hant: rubber-tracks-zh-hant/tbr-tires-zh-hant/agricultural-tractor-tires); getIndustryBySlug is locale-scoped (data.ts:369-372) so locale-suffixed hubs are never built while RubberHeader.astro:143 (and also the mobile menu at :214) links the locale slug → 404. [slug].astro:22 uses tire.industries[0].slug (locale-resolved via the `${locale}::${slug}` index, data.ts:239-249), and the breadcrumb at :115 links the same unbuilt hub. Mechanism fully confirmed; the mobile-menu instance at RubberHeader:214 is an additional uncited occurrence.

findings.title: 'event' resource type is hardwired through nav and filters but the pipeline can never produce it; events.json is orphaned

findings.title: pageText fallbacks duplicate Notion content values and have already drifted
findings.evidence: c:/Users/kappa/Documents/Projects/maxam-tires/src/pages/[locale]/index.astro:60-61 — `backgroundImage={pageText(homePage, 'hero.background_image', '/images/hero-home.jpg')}` `backgroundPosition={pageText(homePage, 'hero.background_position', 'center bottom')}` — pages.json en stores 'center' (not 'center bottom'): the fallback already disagrees with the content it shadows. Text keys use the silent `''` default (data.ts:536-539), so a failed/partial sync renders fallback imagery with EMPTY headings/CTAs — masking pipeline breakage instead of failing the build.
findings.planRef: User decision 3 — snapshots are the single source; fallbacks create double-maintenance the decision exists to prevent
findings.recommendation: Remove content-value fallbacks from pageText call sites; make the build fail loudly (or the section not render) when a required key is missing from the snapshot. Keep fallbacks only for genuinely optional keys, and never as a second copy of a value that lives in Notion.

findings.title: Image pipeline split: 5 repo-committed images referenced from Notion values; all other imagery hotlinks the legacy WordPress site
findings.evidence: public/images contains exactly 5 files (hero-home.jpg, bg-sustainability.jpg, bg-newsletter.jpg, promo-find-your-grip.png, promo-rubber-tracks.jpg); all 5 appear as VALUES inside Notion snapshots (pages.json 'hero.background_image': '/images/hero-home.jpg' etc.; page-promos.json 'image': '/images/promo-rubber-tracks.jpg') — so 'Notion-controlled' imagery actually requires a repo commit to change, and the same paths are re-hardcoded in markup (products/index.astro:30) and fallbacks (index.astro:60/143/187). Everything else hotlinks the live WP site: industries.json `"bgImage": "https://maxamtire.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/11/OTR-01.jpg"`, products.json `"featuredImage": "https://maxamtire.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/12/MS963R_shadow.png"`, articles.json likewise — hundreds of images that all break the day the WP site is decommissioned.
findings.planRef: Decision 3 (Notion is the live source; WP artifacts are one-time migration inputs) — hotlinking WP keeps the old site as a hidden runtime dependency
findings.recommendation: Decide an asset story: mirror WP uploads to the repo/Netlify (or a CDN bucket) during sync and rewrite URLs in snapshots. Document that /images/* values inside Notion must correspond to committed files, or move those assets to Notion-hosted files the sync downloads.
findings.verifierNote: public/images contains exactly the 5 named files. All 5 confirmed as snapshot VALUES: pages.json stores /images/hero-home.jpg, /images/bg-sustainability.jpg, /images/bg-newsletter.jpg; page-promos.json stores /images/promo-rubber-tracks.jpg and /images/promo-find-your-grip.png. Re-hardcoding confirmed at products/index.astro:30 and index.astro:60/143/187. WP hotlinking is if anything understated: products.json 351/351 featuredImage URLs are maxamtire.com/wp-content (including the exact quoted MS963R_shadow.png URL), articles.json 630/630 featured images, industries.json 24/30 bgImages (sample 'OTR-01.jpg' matches the quote exactly) — roughly 1,000 WP-hosted URLs, not just 'hundreds'.

result.summary: The translation-file layer is in excellent shape (121 keys, perfect parity across en/ar-ae/zh-hant, locale-scoped data layer, structure-based block splitting on the tire page that explicitly fixed English-text matching). But the URL/routing layer is critically broken for non-English locales: industry pages are generated from English slugs only, so 5 of the localized industry pages the header links to were never built (404), and the language switcher does a blind prefix swap that 404s on the 11 slug-divergent products and 51 en-only articles — while the trid/translationIds data synced precisely to solve this is consumed by nothing. RTL works today but is split between central [dir='rtl'] CSS and Tailwind rtl:/ms-/me- utilities in 12+ markup files, meaning the decreed Tailwind removal will silently break RTL mirroring; the zh-hant locale is contaminated with Simplified Chinese (3 translation strings, 2 inline region names, an SC font stack, and a stale zh-hans page dir). Salvageable: the fixes are concentrated in routing/slug-mapping and a cleanup pass, not in the content pipeline.
result.stats: 3 locales x 121 keys, 100% key parity; 50/121 keys orphaned (38 category.* + 12 literal); 12 markup files use Tailwind rtl:/logical utilities vs 9 central [dir='rtl'] rule sites (35 logical-property uses vs 3 physical); ~20 hardcoded English aria-labels; 11/117 product trid groups + 3/10 industry slugs locale-divergent; 5 localized industry routes never built (2 ar-ae, 3 zh-hant); 51/358 articles and 6/6 events en-only; 0 hreflang/alternate links; trid/translationIds synced but never consumed; 3 Simplified-Chinese strings inside zh-hant.json; 3 stale empty dirs in src/pages (en, ar-ae, zh-hans)

findings.title: Industry pages built from English slugs only — localized industry routes 404 from the main nav in ar-ae and zh-hant
findings.evidence: src/pages/[locale]/products/[industry]/index.astro:18-27: `const industries = getAllIndustries('en');` then `for (const locale of locales) { for (const industry of industries) { paths.push({ params: { locale, industry: industry.slug } }); } }` — EN slugs are reused for every locale. But industry slugs are locale-specific in src/data/notion-content/industries.json: en has `rubber-tracks`, `tbr-tires`, `agricultural-tires`; ar-ae has `rubber-tracks-ar-ae`, `tbr-ar-ae`; zh-hant has `rubber-tracks-zh-hant`, `tbr-tires-zh-hant`, `agricultural-tractor-tires`. The live header links industries with locale slugs — src/components/global/RubberHeader.astro:143 `href={localePath(locale, `/products/${ind.slug}/`)}` (also :214) — so /ar-ae/products/tbr-ar-ae/ and 4 other localized industry URLs are 404s in the static build. The EN-slug routes that DO get built fail lookup at index.astro:30-34 (`getIndustryBySlug(locale, industrySlug)` → undefined → `Astro.redirect(localePath(locale, '/products'))`). Tire-detail breadcrumbs also link the locale slug: src/pages/[locale]/products/[industry]/[slug].astro:115 `href: localePath(locale, `/products/${primaryIndustry.slug}`)` → 404 for affected industries. Home promos store the EN path for all locales (page-promos.json ar-ae/zh-hant ctaHref `/products/rubber-tracks`) → degrades to a redirect, losing the Rubber Tracks scope.
findings.planRef: User decision 6 (locale layouts identical in structure, no English-matching logic) and docs/wp-page-parity.md page parity
findings.recommendation: Generate [industry] paths per locale from getAllIndustries(locale) (mirroring how [slug].astro already does it at lines 15-27). Then fix cross-locale hrefs: either store locale-correct paths in Notion promos or resolve industry/product hrefs through a trid-based slug map.
findings.verifierNote: Every citation verified exactly. src/pages/[locale]/products/[industry]/index.astro:18-27 builds paths from getAllIndustries('en') for all 3 locales. industries.json confirms exactly 5 divergent localized slugs (ar-ae: rubber-tracks-ar-ae, tbr-ar-ae; zh-hant: rubber-tracks-zh-hant, tbr-tires-zh-hant, agricultural-tractor-tires), so '/ar-ae/products/tbr-ar-ae/ and 4 other localized URLs' is the exact count. RubberHeader.astro:143 and :214 link `localePath(locale, `/products/${ind.slug}/`)` with locale slugs, and RubberHeader IS the live header — BaseLayout.astro:32 renders it on every page (despite RubberHeader's own docstring at lines 19-20 calling itself a 'parallel prototype', that comment is stale). The built EN-slug routes fail getIndustryBySlug(locale, slug) (data.ts:369-372, locale-scoped) and hit the redirect at index.astro:32-34. [slug].astro:115 breadcrumb link confirmed. page-promos.json stores ctaHref '/products/rubber-tracks' for all 3 locales and [locale]/index.astro:85 renders it via localePath, so ar-ae/zh-hant promos land on the meta-refresh redirect page and lose the Rubber Tracks scope. Also verified the recommendation's premise: [slug].astro:15-27 already iterates getAllProducts per locale.

findings.title: Language switcher does a blind locale-prefix swap; trid/translationIds exist in every snapshot but are consumed by nothing — switching 404s on divergent slugs and untranslated content; zero hreflang
findings.evidence: src/components/global/LanguageSwitcher.astro:13-19: `const segments = path.split('/').filter(Boolean); if (locales.includes(segments[0] as Locale)) { segments.shift(); } return `/${targetLocale}/${segments.join('/')}`;` — keeps the rest of the path verbatim. Snapshot data shows 11/117 product trid groups with divergent slugs (e.g. en `ms910r-con` vs ar-ae/zh-hant `ms910r`, en `ms306-plus` vs `ms306-2`), 51 articles en-only (358 en vs 307 ar-ae/zh-hant), 6 events en-only — switching on any of those pages 404s (and there is no custom src/pages/404). Every row carries the WPML translation linkage (src/lib/data.ts:64-65 `trid`, :155/:171 `translationIds`) but `grep -rn "trid|translationIds" src --include=*.astro` returns zero consumers. Query strings are also dropped: RubberHeader.astro:108 passes `currentPath={Astro.url.pathname}` only, so /resources?type=news loses its filter on switch. BaseLayout.astro:23-30 head contains only charset/viewport/description/icons/title — no <link rel="alternate" hreflang>, no canonical, no og:locale anywhere.
findings.planRef: none/undocumented (switcher behavior); decision 3 implies the synced translation linkage should drive cross-locale mapping
findings.recommendation: Build a trid-based translation map at sync or build time; have LanguageSwitcher resolve the actual translated URL (fall back to the target-locale home when no translation exists) and emit hreflang alternates from the same map in BaseLayout.
findings.verifierNote: Core evidence exact: LanguageSwitcher.astro:13-19 does a verbatim prefix swap; re-ran the snapshot analysis — 117 product trid groups, exactly 11 with divergent slugs including the cited ms910r-con/ms910r and ms306-plus/ms306-2 examples; articles 358 en vs 307 ar-ae vs 307 zh-hant; 6 events all language 'en'; grep for trid|translationIds across src/**/*.astro returns zero (only type declarations in data.ts:64/155/171 and sync-side fetchers.ts); Glob finds no 404 page; RubberHeader.astro:108 (and :276) pass only Astro.url.pathname so query strings are dropped; BaseLayout.astro:23-30 head has no hreflang/og:locale/canonical. Two small corrections: (1) en-only article trid groups number 56, not 51 — the 51 was the 358−307 subtraction, so the issue is slightly understated; (2) 'no canonical anywhere' is technically off by one — the root redirect stub src/pages/index.astro:8 has <link rel="canonical" href="/en/">, but no locale content page emits canonical/hreflang/og:locale. Neither correction weakens the finding.

findings.title: RTL mirroring depends on Tailwind rtl:/logical utilities in markup — the decreed Tailwind removal will silently break RTL
findings.evidence: src/styles/global.css:1 `@import 'tailwindcss';` with tailwindcss 4.2.1 + @tailwindcss/vite still in package.json and astro.config.mjs. `rtl:scale-x-[-1]` appears in 12 files, including supposedly converted components: src/components/ui/RubberArticleCard.astro:98 `class="rubber-article__cta-arrow rtl:scale-x-[-1]"`, RubberBreadcrumb.astro:40, RubberProductCard.astro:115, SectionHeader.astro:41, RubberHeader.astro:134/168, ProductCard.astro:69, IndustryCard.astro:29, pages/[locale]/products/[industry]/[slug].astro:200 `class="inline-block rtl:scale-x-[-1] me-1"`, resources/[slug].astro:82. Logical-spacing utilities (ms-/me-/ps-/pe-) in 6 more spots (e.g. RubberHeader.astro:107 `class="ms-2 ps-3 border-s ..."`). The hybrid is institutionalized in comments: src/styles/components/breadcrumb.css:9 "RTL mirror via the rtl:scale-x-[-1] utility on the markup." BaseLayout.astro:31 `<body class="min-h-screen flex flex-col text-maxam-gray-900" ...>` and the entire resources index page (resources/index.astro:28-92) are pure Tailwind with no styles/pages/resources.css. LanguageSwitcher.astro:23-26 is also all utilities.
findings.planRef: User decision 1 (NO Tailwind; semantic class names + central CSS only; no utility classes in .astro markup)
findings.recommendation: Before removing Tailwind, move every arrow/glyph mirror into the component stylesheets as `[dir='rtl'] .x__arrow { transform: scaleX(-1); }` (the pattern already used in testimonial-card.css:68 and tire-gallery.css:225), replace ms-/me- with margin-inline in CSS, and convert resources/index.astro + LanguageSwitcher + BaseLayout body to semantic classes.
findings.verifierNote: Verified: global.css:1 `@import 'tailwindcss'`; tailwindcss 4.2.1 at package.json:25/28 and @tailwindcss/vite wired in astro.config.mjs:3/14. All named rtl:scale-x-[-1] line citations exact (RubberArticleCard:98, RubberBreadcrumb:40, RubberProductCard:115, SectionHeader:41, RubberHeader:134/168, ProductCard:69, IndustryCard:29, [industry]/[slug].astro:200, resources/[slug].astro:82) plus breadcrumb.css:9 comment. Minor count correction: the utility appears in 11 files, not 12 (10 .astro + 1 CSS comment) — though the finding missed ArticleCard.astro:79, which also has it. RubberHeader.astro:107 `ms-2 ps-3 border-s` confirmed; BaseLayout.astro:31 Tailwind body class confirmed; resources/index.astro:28-92 is pure utilities with no styles/pages/resources.css (src/styles/pages contains only home.css, products.css, tire.css); LanguageSwitcher.astro:23-26 all utilities. The recommendation's cited CSS patterns exist ([dir='rtl'] scaleX(-1) at testimonial-card.css ~67-69 and tire-gallery.css ~224-227). Additional supporting evidence the finding missed: resources/index.astro's client script toggles Tailwind classes at runtime (lines 223-231, e.g. classList.toggle('bg-maxam-blue')), so Tailwind removal also breaks filter-button active states there.

findings.title: 50 of 121 translation keys are orphans; localizeCategory is dead code with a latent raw-key-rendering bug
findings.evidence: Usage scan (literal + template t() calls across src): 12 literal orphans — nav.search, products.filter.type, products.applications, products.pagination.page, product.features, product.downloads, home.industries_subheading, home.resources_subheading, industry.products_heading, industry.view_all_products, resources.filter.industry, common.learn_more — plus the entire 38-key category.* namespace, whose only consumer is localizeCategory (src/lib/i18n.ts:52-64), which is never called anywhere (grep finds only the definition); taxonomy names actually come Notion-localized via data.ts toIndustry/toApplication (`name: row.name`). i18n.ts:61 references `category.${group}.${obj.id}.description` — no `.description` key exists in any locale, so t() would render the raw key (i18n.ts:15 `?? key`). The category keys have also drifted from data: no key for industrial-forklift-tires, and keys use EN slugs that don't exist in ar/zh. Distrust-fallback dead code confirms the confusion: [slug].astro:187 `{t(locale, 'product.documents') || 'Documents'}` and resources/[slug].astro:39 `t(locale, `resources.type.${article.type}`) || article.type` — t() never returns a falsy value.
findings.planRef: Decision 4 (translations are for UI chrome) — category names are editorial content that already flows from Notion per decision 3
findings.recommendation: Delete localizeCategory and the category.* namespace; prune or wire up the 12 literal orphans; remove the dead || fallbacks.
findings.verifierNote: Independently reproduced the scan: en.json has exactly 121 keys; category.* namespace is exactly 38 keys; literal+template usage scan across all src .astro/.ts files yields exactly the 12 cited literal orphans (the only dynamic t() prefixes are resources.type., language., and category. — the last existing only inside localizeCategory). localizeCategory (i18n.ts:52-64) is referenced in no file other than its definition. data.ts:204/214 confirm taxonomy names come from Notion (name: row.name). One wording correction: a '.description' key does exist in each locale — site.description (en.json:3, used by BaseLayout) — but no category.*.description key exists anywhere, which is what i18n.ts:61 constructs, so the latent raw-key-rendering claim stands as scoped. Also confirmed: no category.industry.* key for industrial-forklift-tires (9 keys vs 10 industries), and the keys use EN slugs absent in ar/zh (rubber-tracks vs rubber-tracks-ar-ae). Dead fallbacks verified verbatim at [industry]/[slug].astro:187 and resources/[slug].astro:39; t() (i18n.ts:14-16) ends in `?? key` and can never return falsy.

result.summary: The user's suspicion is confirmed and quantifiable: across 8 active page templates there are 5 distinct container/gutter implementations, 5 active hero/page-header treatments (plus 1 dead Hero component), 6 distinct h1 scales, 4 section-spacing systems, and 4+ grid implementations. The site is split into two generations: home, products index, industry hub, and tire detail were migrated to the semantic central-CSS system (each with its own container scheme), while resources index, resource detail, and contact were never migrated and remain fully Tailwind-utility-built — including a scoped <style> block on contact — in direct violation of user decision 1. The one genuinely shared atom (RubberBreadcrumb) is deployed on only 3 of 7 public templates with 3 different wrappers and 2 different root-crumb conventions. Both design docs (wp-page-parity.md, DESIGN-SYSTEM.md) and the in-repo design gallery reference components that don't exist or claims that are no longer true.
result.stats: 9 templates audited (8 locale + root redirect). Container systems: 5 (Section/section__container; products-page/--page-gutter; .tire-page ad hoc; Tailwind max-w-[var(--container-max)] px-4 on resources-index/contact/design/Footer; Tailwind max-w-3xl on resource detail). Hero systems: 5 active + 1 dead (Hero.astro). h1 treatments: 6. Section-spacing systems: 4 (+2 dead spacing tokens). Breadcrumb: 1 component, used on 3/7 public templates, 3 wrappers, 2 crumb-root conventions, absent on 3. Pages CSS files: 3 (home/products/tire) covering only 4 of 8 templates. Tailwind-built public templates: 3/7. Tailwind utility class attrs: resources-index 15, contact 11 (+37-line scoped style), resource-detail 10, design 199, vs 0-1 on migrated pages.

'bg-maxam-blue text-maxam-white border-maxam-blue' : ...}``. src/pages/[locale]/resources/[slug].astro:43 `<article class="max-w-3xl mx-auto px-4 py-10">`, :61 `<h1 class="text-4xl font-bold text-maxam-black leading-tight">`. src/pages/[locale]/contact/index.astro:65 `<section class="max-w-[var(--container-max)] mx-auto px-4 py-10">`, :77 `class="flex flex-col lg:flex-row gap-10"`, :95 `class="w-full lg:w-[420px] shrink-0"`, and lines 146-182 a scoped `<style>` defining `.rubber-contact-form` (also a 'rubber' class-name violation). findings.planRef: User decision 1 (NO Tailwind; semantic classes + central CSS only; no scoped <style>; pages are pure markup) and decision 2 (no rubber prefix)
findings.recommendation: Create src/styles/pages/resources.css, resource-detail.css, contact.css and migrate the three templates to semantic classes following the home/tire pattern; move the contact-form panel styles out of the scoped block; replace JS-injected pagination classnames with state attributes (e.g. data-active) styled in CSS. findings.title: 5 parallel container/gutter systems; content edges do not align across pages or even within one page
findings.evidence: (1) Canonical: src/styles/components/section.css:110-116 `.section__container { max-width: var(--container-max); margin-inline: auto; padding-inline: clamp(1rem, 4vw, 2rem); }` — used by home and the industry hub's bottom section. (2) Full-bleed: src/styles/pages/products.css:19-22 `.products-page { padding-inline: var(--page-gutter); }` with global.css:128-136 `--page-gutter: clamp(1rem,4vw,2rem)` jumping to `8vw` at ≥1280px — products index + industry hub. (3) Ad hoc re-implementation: src/styles/pages/tire.css:12-16 `.tire-page { max-width: var(--container-max); margin-inline: auto; padding: 2.5rem clamp(1rem, 4vw, 2rem) 4rem; }` — duplicates section__container instead of using Section. (4) Tailwind fixed-gutter: resources/index.astro:28, contact/index.astro:65, design/index.astro:102, and Footer.astro:16 `max-w-[var(--container-max)] mx-auto px-4` — px-4 is a fixed 1rem, so these pages' content edge sits up to 1rem/side outside the clamp-gutter pages at desktop widths. (5) Unique narrow column: resources/[slug].astro:43 `max-w-3xl` (768px), used nowhere else. Within a single page, products/[industry]/index.astro mixes systems 2 and 1: the viewer at :85 uses `.products-page` (8vw gutters on wide screens) while the related-articles block at :101 uses `<Section background="sunken">` (centered 1280px), so the two blocks' edges misalign on viewports where 8vw gutters exceed the container gutter.
findings.planRef: User decision 5 (content in centered 1280px container; only backgrounds bleed; full-bleed products/header use --page-gutter)
findings.recommendation: Decision 5 sanctions exactly two systems (Section container + --page-gutter full-bleed for products/header). Migrate tire detail to Section (or have .tire-page consume the same primitive), replace the Tailwind containers on resources/contact/footer with Section or a shared .page-container class using the clamp gutter, and decide deliberately whether resource detail's 768px reading column is a sanctioned third width — if so, name it as a token (e.g. --container-prose) instead of max-w-3xl.
findings.verifierNote: All five systems verified at cited lines: section.css:110-116 .section__container exact; products.css:19-22 .products-page with global.css:128-136 --page-gutter (clamp(1rem,4vw,2rem) → 8vw at ≥1280px) exact; tire.css:12-16 re-implements the same max-width/margin-inline/clamp padding instead of using Section, exact; the four Tailwind `max-w-[var(--container-max)] mx-auto px-4` containers verified at resources/index.astro:28, contact/index.astro:65, design/index.astro:102, Footer.astro:16 (px-4 = fixed 1rem vs the 2rem clamp ceiling — the up-to-1rem/side edge offset claim is correct, since box-sizing:border-box means content width differs even when the 1280px cap is hit); resources/[slug].astro:43 max-w-3xl verified. Minor nuance: the max-w-3xl class string also appears on a paragraph in the internal design gallery (design/index.astro:109), but as a page reading column it is unique to resource detail. The within-page mixing on the industry hub is verified ([industry]/index.astro:85 .products-page vs :101 Section background="sunken"); if anything the finding understates it — the two edges misalign at essentially all wide viewports (in both directions depending on width), not only where 8vw exceeds the container gutter.

findings.evidence: RubberBreadcrumb is used on 3 of 7 public templates with different wrappers and positions: industry hub renders it BELOW the hero in its own gutter block, products/[industry]/index.astro:75-81 `<div class="products-breadcrumb">` (products.css:26-29, --page-gutter); tire detail renders it ABOVE the title inside the container, [slug].astro:112-118 `<div class="tire-page__breadcrumb">` (tire.css:18-20); resource detail wraps it in a raw Tailwind div, resources/[slug].astro:45 `<div class="mb-6">`. result.summary: The data layer itself is well-built: clean snapshot-backed accessors, no Notion API leakage into the Astro build, sensible memory strategy (31MB of block sidecars fs-read lazily, row snapshots statically imported). But it rests on a false contract — "slugs are locale-stable" — which is untrue for industries, producing broken navigation and missing pages in ar-ae/zh-hant (2 of 3 locales). The Notion renderers and detail pages are still Tailwind-dependent despite the NO-Tailwind decision, and a thick crust of dead code (mock data, dead exports, an entire events pipeline that reaches no real page, stale Tire/SpecTable types) misrepresents what the system actually does. Salvageable, but the slug-identity model and the Tailwind entanglement need planned work before anything else.
result.stats: data.ts: 25 exported functions — 19 used by real pages/nav, 2 used only by the /design playground (getAllRawDocuments, getAllEvents), 2 dead (getAllDocuments, getArticlesByType), plus getProductBlocks/getArticleBlocks live and readBlocks('event') unreachable. i18n.ts: 7 exports, 1 dead (localizeCategory). src/lib/notion/*: 4 files, imported ONLY by scripts/sync-from-notion.ts — zero runtime leakage. Snapshots: 351 products, 972 articles, 390 documents, 30 industries, 21 testimonials, 6 events, 3 pages, 6 promos; 1328 block sidecars (31MB, lazily fs-read) vs ~8MB statically imported (4.8MB of it WP-derived product-specs). Block-type coverage: 11/11 types observed in sidecars are handled. Dead mock data: 699 lines.

findings.title: Industry routes built from EN slugs while industry slugs are locale-specific — broken nav/breadcrumbs and missing pages in ar-ae and zh-hant
findings.evidence: src/pages/[locale]/products/[industry]/index.astro:18-27 generates paths from English slugs for ALL locales: `const industries = getAllIndustries('en'); ... for (const locale of locales) { for (const industry of industries) { paths.push({ params: { locale, industry: industry.slug } }); } }`. But src/data/notion-content/industries.json slugs differ per locale: en has `agricultural-tires, rubber-tracks, tbr-tires`; ar-ae has `rubber-tracks-ar-ae, tbr-ar-ae`; zh-hant has `agricultural-tractor-tires, rubber-tracks-zh-hant, tbr-tires-zh-hant`. At render, index.astro:30-34 does `getIndustryBySlug(locale, industrySlug); if (!industry) { return Astro.redirect(...) }` — so e.g. /zh-hant/products/agricultural-tires/ is an empty redirect stub, and /zh-hant/products/agricultural-tractor-tires/ is NEVER generated. Meanwhile every link uses the locale-correct slug: RubberIndustryCard.astro:23 `const href = `/${locale}/products/${industry.slug}/``, RubberHeader.astro:143+214, the tire-page breadcrumb [slug].astro:115 `href: localePath(locale, `/products/${primaryIndustry.slug}`)`, and the Notion promo card ctaHref `/products/rubber-tracks` (page-promos.json, all locales) localePath'd at index.astro:85. Verified zh-hant products carry zh-hant industry slugs (products.json: `zh-hant tracxtra → ["agricultural-tractor-tires"]`), so product detail URLs exist under industry segments whose landing page 404s.
findings.planRef: User decision 6 (locale layouts identical, no English-matching logic); contradicts data.ts:561-562 comment "Page is identified by its slug (which is locale-stable)" and fetchers.ts:7-8 "translates these to slugs (language-stable identity)"
findings.recommendation: Pick one slug-identity model and enforce it: either (a) make slugs truly locale-stable in Notion (rename ar-ae/zh-hant industry slugs to match en, drop the -ar-ae/-zh-hant suffixes), or (b) generate [industry] paths per-locale from getAllIndustries(locale) and resolve cross-locale identity via trid. Add a build-time assertion that every industry slug referenced by a product/link resolves to a generated route.
findings.verifierNote: Re-verified every citation. [industry]/index.astro:18-27 generates paths from getAllIndustries('en') for all locales; :30-34 redirects unknown slugs. industries.json slugs confirmed locale-specific exactly as quoted (ar-ae differs in 2: rubber-tracks-ar-ae, tbr-ar-ae — it shares agricultural-tires with en; zh-hant differs in 3 incl. agricultural-tractor-tires). products.json confirms zh-hant tracxtra → ["agricultural-tractor-tires"], so its detail page is generated under an industry segment whose landing page is never built, and the breadcrumb at [slug].astro:115 links there → 404. RubberIndustryCard.astro:23 href quote exact. page-promos.json ctaHref '/products/rubber-tracks' in all 3 locales confirmed. Citation corrections: the header is src/components/global/RubberHeader.astro (not ui/), with locale-slug chip links at ~143 and ~214 as claimed; the 'locale-stable' comment is at data.ts:557-558 (not 561-562); fetchers is src/lib/notion/fetchers.ts:8. Substance fully holds.

findings.title: Block sidecars are a month staler than the row snapshots, and missing sidecars fail silently to an empty page body
findings.evidence: _manifest.json: `"syncedAt":"2026-06-10T15:56:08.524Z","withBlocks":false` — the latest sync skipped bodies; blocks/* files date from May 3-4 (ls -lt). data.ts:49-57 readBlocks: `if (!existsSync(file)) return []; try { return JSON.parse(...) } catch { return []; }` — missing or corrupt sidecar silently yields an empty body. Concretely: article `en::how-weather-extremes-affect-construction-tire-maintenance` (articles.json, publishedDate 2024-02-28, no externalLink) has NO sidecar, so its detail page builds and ships with a header and zero body today. This matches the page layer's uniform silent-fallback philosophy: pageText returns '' (data.ts:536-539), getProductSpecs returns undefined (data.ts:610), missing slugs become redirect stubs ([slug].astro:32-34, [industry]/index.astro:32-34), t() falls back en→raw-key (i18n.ts:14-16). The only hard errors in the whole pipeline are sync-from-notion.ts:122-143 (empty Pages/Promos throws).
findings.planRef: User decision 3 (snapshots are the only live data source) — staleness between two halves of the same snapshot is undocumented
findings.recommendation: Make readBlocks distinguish 'sidecar legitimately absent (external article)' from 'sidecar missing' and fail or warn at build for the latter. Record withBlocks state per content row in the manifest and have the build refuse (or loudly warn) when rendering detail pages from a --without-blocks sync.
findings.verifierNote: _manifest.json quote exact (syncedAt 2026-06-10T15:56:08.524Z, withBlocks:false); all 1328 sidecars have May 3-4 mtimes (newest May 4 09:05). readBlocks (data.ts:49-57) silent-empty behavior quoted exactly. One correction: 'the only hard errors' claim is slightly off — sync-from-notion.ts throws at lines 116/123/132/139 (the cited 122-143 covers the two empty-DB throws but not the two missing-config throws at 116/132) and src/lib/notion/client.ts:32 throws on missing NOTION_TOKEN. Two minor corrections: the mock helper functions span lines 548-565 (not 549-561), and only getAllSizes/getAllRatings shadow data.ts names — getTireBySlug has no data.ts counterpart (data.ts exports getProductBySlug).

findings.title: Domain types in src/types/index.ts no longer match what the data layer produces: Tire.features/specifications/specTable/description are never populated; SpecTable survives only via a hardcoded design-page fallback
findings.evidence: data.ts toTire always emits `description: ''` (line 245, comment 'body lives in Notion blocks'), `features: []` (262), `specifications: []` (263), `galleryImages: []` (265) and never sets specTable or specialLogo — yet types/index.ts:49-67 declares all of them on Tire, and the old SpecTable interface (types/index.ts:34-37) plus SpecRow (28-31) persist alongside the NEW ProductSpecs/SpecField/SpecVariant defined in data.ts:581-596 (a parallel spec model). Consequences: design/index.astro:61 `getAllProducts(locale).filter((p) => p.specTable && p.specTable.rows.length > 0)` is ALWAYS empty, so the SpecTable specimen always renders the hardcoded `fallbackSpecTable` (design/index.astro:62-70); ProductCard.astro:61 renders `{tire.description}` which is always ''. Also Document.type 'compliance' is never produced (documents.json types are only product-sheet/brochure; toDocument coerces at data.ts:227-229), Document.tireIds is never set, and ArticleType 'event' is never produced. The only populater of the stale fields is dead mock-tires.ts.
findings.planRef: docs/DESIGN-SYSTEM.md (lists SpecTable as 'Real') — stale; none documents the Tire-type split
findings.recommendation: Prune Tire to what toTire actually emits (drop features/specifications/specTable/specialLogo/description or mark them clearly as block-derived), move ProductSpecs types into src/types, delete SpecTable/SpecRow + SpecTable.astro, and decide whether 'compliance'/'event' enum members have a future.
findings.verifierNote: All citations exact: toTire emits description:'' (data.ts:245), features:[] (262), specifications:[] (263), galleryImages:[] (265) and never sets specTable/specialLogo; types/index.ts declares SpecRow (28-31), SpecTable (34-37), and all stale Tire fields (49-67); the parallel ProductSpecs/SpecField/SpecVariant model lives at data.ts:581-596. design/index.astro:61 filter on p.specTable verified — necessarily always empty — with fallbackSpecTable at :62-70 always winning. ProductCard.astro:61 renders {tire.description} (always ''); note ProductCard itself is only used by the design page, so impact is confined there. documents.json contains only types ['product-sheet','brochure'] so 'compliance' is never produced and toDocument (data.ts:227-229) never sets tireIds; toArticle never emits 'event'. mock-tires.ts confirmed as the only populater of features/specifications/specTable. DESIGN-SYSTEM.md:55 confirmed listing SpecTable as 'Real'.

findings.title: Featured products are hardcoded EN slugs in the homepage; getFeaturedProducts has no notion of 'featured'
findings.evidence: src/pages/[locale]/index.astro:42-47: `const wpFeaturedSlugs = ['agrixtra-85', 'ms453', 'ms600', 'ms709']; ... wpPicked.length === wpFeaturedSlugs.length ? wpPicked : getFeaturedProducts(locale, 4);` — editorial product curation lives in code, not Notion (works today in all 3 locales since these slugs are shared, verified against products.json). The fallback getFeaturedProducts (data.ts:357-361) is a misnomer: it just returns the FIRST N products with an industry (`all.slice(0, limit)`) — no featured flag, no ordering. RubberHeader.astro:24 also uses it for nav 'featured' products, so nav features whatever sorts first in the Notion query.
findings.planRef: User decision 3 (ALL editorial content flows from Notion)
findings.recommendation: Add a Featured checkbox/order property to the Notion Products DB, sync it into the snapshot, and make getFeaturedProducts filter on it. Delete wpFeaturedSlugs.
findings.verifierNote: index.astro:42-47 quoted exactly. Verified all four slugs (agrixtra-85, ms453, ms600, ms709) exist in products.json in all three locales, so the hardcoded path is what actually renders everywhere. getFeaturedProducts (data.ts:357-361) confirmed: filters language + industries.length>0 then slice(0, limit) — no featured flag or ordering, name is a misnomer. RubberHeader citation correction: file is src/components/global/RubberHeader.astro; line 24 is the import, the actual nav usage is line 48 (getFeaturedProducts(locale, 1)[0], rendered via RubberFeaturedCard at :154/:222). Substance fully holds.

findings.title: Two parallel Notion clients with two different .env loaders
findings.evidence: src/lib/notion/client.ts:14-35 (hand-rolled .env parser + `export const notion = new Client({ auth: token })`, throws at module load if NOTION_TOKEN unset) is used only by scripts/sync-from-notion.ts. scripts/notion-client.ts:1-14 (dotenv-based, also requires NOTION_PARENT_PAGE_ID, plus decodeEntities/rewriteMediaUrl helpers) is used by 25 migration scripts. Both construct independent Client singletons and both read scripts/output/notion-ids.json conventions. No runtime leakage either way: grep confirms zero imports of lib/notion from src/pages, src/components, src/layouts, or data.ts — the Astro build is genuinely offline (the module-level NOTION_TOKEN throw in client.ts would in fact make any accidental page import fail the build loudly, which is an accidental but real guard).
findings.planRef: none/undocumented
findings.recommendation: Since the 25 consumers are one-time migration artifacts, fold what survives into src/lib/notion/client.ts (or move both under scripts/) and standardize on one env-loading path. Low priority; do it when the migration scripts get archived.
findings.verifierNote: minor — not verified

findings.title: data.ts API inconsistencies: misleading names, unscoped signatures, presentation logic in the data layer, and a memory strategy that contradicts its own rationale
findings.evidence: (a) getAllDocuments (data.ts:425) returns Article[] (adapter shape) while getAllRawDocuments (433) returns Document[] — the 'plain' name returns the adapted shape, inverted from every other getAllX. (b) getAllSizes/getAllRatings (497-507) take NO locale and aggregate across all three locales' product rows, breaking the `(locale, ...)` signature convention used by all 20+ other accessors (harmless today because sizes/ratings are locale-invariant, but undocumented). (c) toEvent (459-468) hardcodes a URL `href: `/${locale}/resources/${e.slug}`` inside the data layer instead of letting pages use i18n.localePath — the only place data.ts does routing. (d) data.ts:39-46 justifies fs-sidecars to 'avoid statically importing tens of MB' yet lines 33-35 statically import 4.8MB of product-specs JSON. (e) data.ts:431-432 docstring says 'for callers that want to render a RubberDocumentCard' — the banned Rubber naming, in the data layer's contract docs.
findings.planRef: User decision 2 (no Rubber naming) for (e); rest none/undocumented
findings.recommendation: Rename getAllDocuments→getDocumentsAsResources (or delete it — it's dead), add locale params or an explicit comment to getAllSizes/getAllRatings, drop href from Event, and scrub Rubber from comments. Consider lazy-loading specs per-locale if build memory ever matters.
findings.verifierNote: minor — not verified

findings.title: NotionBlocks claims unknown blocks 'fall back to a debug comment' but silently drops them; current coverage is complete only by luck
findings.evidence: NotionBlocks.astro:8-9 header comment: 'Unknown block types fall back to a debug comment so we notice them without breaking the page' — but the default case (208-211) is `// Unknown block — emit an HTML comment so we can spot it in dev ... return null;` — returns null, emits nothing. Verified against reality: scanning all 1328 sidecar files yields exactly {paragraph:4017, heading_2:1324, image:1391, bulleted_list_item:2887, heading_3:805, divider:53, numbered_list_item:167, heading_1:1, quote:3, table:351, table_row:6287} — every one handled (table_row via tableRows(), NotionBlocks.astro:47-49), and the callout/toggle/code/column_list handlers are currently exercised by zero blocks. So nothing is dropped TODAY, but the first editor who adds a video/embed/bookmark block in Notion will see content vanish with no signal.
findings.planRef: NotionBlocks.astro's own header comment (8-9)
findings.recommendation: Make the default case emit a real HTML comment (or a build-time console.warn with slug+type), matching the documented intent. Cheap insurance for editor-driven content.
findings.verifierNote: minor — not verified

findings.evidence: src/pages/ contains empty directories `en/`, `ar-ae/`, and `zh-hans/` (created Mar 15, verified empty via ls -la) alongside the live `[locale]/` tree. `zh-hans` is Simplified Chinese — the site's locale is `zh-hant` (i18n.ts:34 `locales = ['en', 'ar-ae', 'zh-hant']`).
